title: Rewrite README.md as concise GitHub landing page linking to docs/NBNv2.md
description:
## Summary
Rewrite repository root `README.md` to be a standard GitHub landing page with concise project positioning and clear links into the full docs.

## Precondition
This issue starts only after both docs split and docs render automation issues are complete.

## Scope
- Rewrite `README.md` with typical GitHub structure and concise content:
  - project summary and goals
  - high-level architecture snapshot
  - quick start/build/test commands
  - local demo pointers
  - status/roadmap notes (brief)
  - contribution/development notes (brief)
- Include explicit link to `docs/NBNv2.md` as the full specification.
- Include link to `docs/INDEX.md` as the source template for spec assembly.
- Respect canonical docs layout messaging:
  - cross-cutting docs in repo-root `docs/sections/*`
  - project-specific docs adjacent to code in `src/*/Design.md`, `tools/*/Design.md`, `tests/*/Design.md`
  - no project-level `Docs/` or `docs/` subfolders

## Required implementation details
1. Keep README concise and newcomer-friendly; avoid full-spec duplication.
2. Ensure command examples match repository defaults (`Release`, `--disable-build-servers`).
3. Ensure links are valid in GitHub web view.
4. Ensure README language stays accurate with current repo structure and project names.

## Verification checklist
- README renders cleanly on GitHub markdown preview.
- All links resolve.
- New contributor can discover full spec and local demo path within first screenful.

## Out of scope
- Spec content itself (handled by docs split/render issues).
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Rewrote root README into a concise GitHub landing page. Added explicit links to docs/NBNv2.md and docs/INDEX.md, kept architecture/build-test/demo/status/contribution sections brief, and aligned command defaults to Release + --disable-build-servers. Verified markdown links resolve and docs render freshness check passes.

title: Investigate repeated deep single-branch lineage splitting in live speciation
description: Live reruns after the singleton/bootstrap fixes still show one lineage that repeatedly produces nested derived species with few or no parallel sibling species at the same depth. Audit runtime/speciation semantics to confirm whether this is expected from the current thresholds/hysteresis/defaults or whether one path is still nudging serial branch-on-branch splits too aggressively.
close_reason: Completed follow-up: divergence no longer prefers the newest derived species inside a single lineage family; regression coverage added and full suite passed (726/726).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Latest live operator note: the repeated deep single-branch split-chain pattern only seems to show up when simulator parent-selection bias is set toward diversity/divergence (possibly neutral as well; not yet confirmed). It does not appear to be a stability-mode default artifact. Keep this as a live-capture clue, not a confirmed root-cause statement.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Reviewed root epic NBNv2-zwv plus NBNv2-zwv.21-.27 before implementation. Conclusion: the remaining repeated deep single-branch split pattern was not coming from the current SpeciationManagerActor bootstrap/realign/reuse guards; it was coming from EvolutionSim still treating each freshly derived species as 'new' for parent bias and pool turnover. Implemented a runtime/tooling fix: speciation commit results now propagate source_species_id into EvolutionSim, simulator tracks lineage-family identity per species, divergence/stability parent bias uses lineage-family age/count when multiple families are present, and pool eviction preserves rarer lineage families by evicting within the dominant family first. Added regression coverage for source-species metadata parsing, lineage-family-aware selection, and family-preserving turnover. Validation: targeted tools/speciation slices passed, then full dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp passed (725/725).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Live follow-up after commit 2519ab2: repeated deep single-branch splitting is still reproducible when parent_selection_bias=divergence. Reopening investigation.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Live follow-up: repeated deep single-branch splitting remains reproducible when parent_selection_bias=divergence; continuing investigation.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up fix after live repro against commit 2519ab2: the lineage-family pass removed cross-family novelty resets, but divergence still preferred the newest derived species when the pool had collapsed to a single lineage family with multiple derived species. Root cause was EvolutionSimulationSession still falling back to species-age recency in that exact case. Implemented the narrower follow-up: divergence now suppresses age weighting when only one lineage family is present and instead spreads picks by species representation within that family, so branch breadth is favored over repeatedly selecting the newest leaf. Added regression coverage for the single-family divergence case and reran validation: targeted tools slice 22/22, broader tools+SpeciationPane slice 57/57, full dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp passed (726/726).

close_reason: Closed after user verified both mixed-topology configurations are now working in thorough live testing following the runtime follow-up fixes.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: User reports two still-open regressions after commit 11ee300. 1) Remote spawn is still broken when this Linux machine runs the core services/Workbench and the only worker is remote; reverse topology works for spawn. 2) In the topology where spawn does work, speciation never triggers even though EvolutionSim runs, the population increases, and manual reproduction works. Investigate real runtime causes; avoid papering over with test-only changes or UI-only masking.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Added two runtime-side follow-up fixes while keeping this issue open for verification. 1) EvolutionSim launch in SpeciationPanel now runs through local firewall-attention handling for its own simulator remoting port, so IO/Reproduction reply-path failures are surfaced directly instead of appearing only as request timeouts. 2) HiveMind sender PID matching now accepts hostname/IP-equivalent endpoints when actor id and port match, closing the likely mixed-topology ack/reconcile timeout path. Workbench spawn/sample status also surfaces artifact-port firewall attention instead of burying it in logs. Verified with focused Workbench+HiveMind tests (154/154) and docs freshness check.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: User validation after 7d480af: both previously broken scenarios worked at least once. One second-to-last run in the topology with this machine as the remote worker still saw spawned brains die/unregister while the worker remained online/ready and energy/cost were disabled. User notes this was before running Workbench elevated on that machine, so the issue may have been related to missing local firewall exceptions and may already be resolved. Holding code changes unless the failure repeats or a clearer signal appears.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Fresh log analysis: the apparent contradiction was real. Worker online/ready status is inventory-driven and could remain green while shard execution was already unhealthy. Latest logs show the actual brain loss came from repeated HiveMind TickCompute/TickDeliver timeouts and failed reschedules on the core-services host, while SettingsMonitor also hit duplicate node_capabilities heartbeat inserts that could leave readiness/capability state misleading. Fixed the clear SettingsMonitor bug by honoring heartbeat timestamps and upserting duplicate (node_id,time_ms) capability rows instead of throwing, with regression coverage. Verified focused SettingsMonitor+Workbench+HiveMind tests (158/158). Issue remains open for the underlying tick-timeout/stall root cause.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Added two runtime-side follow-up fixes for the remaining mixed-topology brain-loss path. 1) BrainSignalRouter now accepts shard delivery ACKs from PID-equivalent endpoints (same actor id + equivalent host/port), matching HiveMind’s existing hostname/IP endpoint tolerance and preventing mixed-topology TickDeliver stalls from strict sender string mismatches. 2) HiveMind no longer lets actor-driven UnregisterBrain from a hosted brain-root/signal-router tear down the tracked brain while placement replacement or recovery is in flight; senderless/system unregister still removes the brain. Added regressions in BrainSignalRouterPidMatchingTests and HiveMindPlacementLifecycleTests. Verified focused Brain + HiveMind suites (66/66) plus docs freshness check. Leaving issue open pending live mixed-topology retest.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Follow-up from the newer run where recovery mostly worked but timeout storms resumed. Latest logs showed recovery/reschedule succeeding, then one recovered brain immediately re-learned its output coordinator as 192.168.0.14:12041/brain-...-output while peers still used 192.168.0.14:12041/worker-node/brain-...-output. Root cause was WorkerNode reconcile fallback: when HostedPid was already null, BuildObservedActorPid synthesized address + actorName without restoring the worker-root actor prefix. HiveMind then updated control/output endpoints to malformed synthetic PIDs after recovery, which can reintroduce timeouts right after a successful reschedule. Fixed WorkerNode reconcile fallback to preserve worker-root ancestry and added a regression that stops the hosted output coordinator before reconcile and asserts the fallback ActorPid remains address/worker-root/child. Verified focused WorkerNode + Brain + HiveMind suites (97/97). Leaving issue open pending another live retest.

title: Investigate slow brain placement and burst spawn timeouts for external-world spawn loops
description:
Investigate the runtime behavior behind slow brain placement and burst spawn failures seen from the Basics external-world demos. This is not yet a proven root cause report; capture the problem as observed and narrow it with runtime instrumentation and focused tests.

Observed behavior from real Basics live trials:
- Conservative AND profiles succeed on one worker, including exact-template runs that improve from gen1 accuracy=0.75 fitness=0.764 to gen2 accuracy=1 fitness=1.
- One-worker XOR can reach 5+ generations without full setup collapse, but it plateaus/oscillates around accuracy=0.5 fitness=0.528 instead of steadily improving.
- Higher-pressure runs can fail generation 1 with client-side spawn timeouts and related setup failures, for example:
  - spawn_request_failed: TimeoutException: Request didn't receive any Response within the expected time.
  - geometry_invalid: brain_info_missing
  - output_timeout_or_width_mismatch on some richer XOR seed/template experiments
- Multi-worker same-host experiments are now possible and discoverable, but they do not automatically eliminate the problem. A 4-worker XOR profile still failed in generation 1 with spawn request timeouts / missing BrainInfo.
- Earlier same-host runs also showed uneven placement concentration (user observed roughly 7 brains on worker 1 and 1 on worker 2 in one run), which motivated the already-landed same-locality hosted-brain-count tie-break change in 0ab0af5.

Important boundary conditions / what we know:
- Some failures are surfacing as client-side timeouts waiting for SpawnBrainViaIOAck, not yet as explicit HiveMind spawn_assignment_timeout/spawn_reconcile_timeout reason codes.
- Worker availability/configuration can be a confounder; for example, a worker launched with the default 80% storage limit was ineligible on a host already at 89.7% disk usage, and raising the worker storage limit to 95% made it eligible.
- The problem therefore may be in one or more of: IO/HiveMind request timeout budgeting, placement/registration latency, worker discovery/bootstrap lag, or poor behavior under fast sequential spawns / small bursts of concurrent SpawnBrainViaIO requests.

Desired outcome:
- Determine where the time is being spent in the SpawnBrainViaIO -> HiveMind placement -> worker assignment/ready -> BrainInfo-visible path during repeated external-world spawn/evaluate/kill loops.
- Add or improve diagnostics so external-world callers can tell the difference between client-side request timeout, placement assignment timeout, reconcile timeout, and worker/bootstrap availability issues.
- If appropriate, make a narrowly scoped runtime change that improves repeated spawn throughput or failure clarity without breaking locality/placement invariants.

Suggested investigation points:
- IO Gateway spawn forwarding timeout and response path
- HiveMind placement planning/execution timing, especially under repeated small brain placements
- worker assignment ack / ready / reconcile timing
- same-host multi-worker behavior, including whether pending/in-flight placements are balanced sanely
- end-to-end SpawnBrainViaIO coverage for timeout/reconcile reason-code translation
close_reason: Primary scope landed; remaining intermittent rapid-spawn stall split to follow-up.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text:
Evidence snapshot from the live Basics investigation:
- AND baseline on one worker is healthy: exact-template potential and buffer runs improved from gen1 accuracy=0.75 fitness=0.764 to gen2 accuracy=1 fitness=1, so the end-to-end spawn/evaluate/kill loop can work under light pressure.
- XOR is the better stress case. One-worker exact-template XOR reached at least generation 6 without total setup collapse, but oscillated between accuracy=0.5 fitness=0.528 and accuracy=0.25 fitness=0.292 instead of trending upward.
- Increasing worker count did not automatically fix the problem. After explicit WorkerNode startup with unique actor names/ports and a 95% storage limit, HiveMind reported EligibleWorkerCount=2 and later EligibleWorkerCount=4, yet tested XOR profiles still failed generation 1 or regressed quickly.
- Failure signatures seen from the external-world side included: spawn_request_failed: TimeoutException waiting for SpawnBrainViaIOAck; geometry_invalid: brain_info_missing; and on some richer seed/template experiments, output_timeout_or_width_mismatch.
- One user-observed same-host distribution example was roughly 7 brains on worker 1 and 1 on worker 2, which is why the hosted-brain-count tie-break was adjusted in 0ab0af5. That change improved the planner heuristic but did not by itself eliminate the burst-spawn issue.
- A worker launched with the default 80% storage limit was initially ineligible on a host already at 89.7% disk usage; rerunning with --storage-pct 95 made it eligible. That is a real confounder and should be separated from actual placement/timeout behavior during investigation.
- The most likely productive instrumentation is along the full SpawnBrainViaIO path: IO request receive time, HiveMind forwarding/queueing, placement planning, worker assignment/ready/reconcile timings, and the point where BrainInfo becomes queryable/visible to IO-side callers.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Resolved the main scope for NBNv2-4mp: queued vs placement-ready spawn acknowledgements, faster placement-visible path, worker-lane queueing/serial dispatch, placement reply routing hardening, GPU threshold calibration/settings, worker-host backend visibility, system-load sparkline/analog health, and designer-managed fast failure cleanup. Remaining intermittent rapid same-worker spawn stalls were split to a follow-up issue before closure.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up fix for empty edge visualization: RegionShard now emits route-addressed VizAxonSent events per outbox destination (source/target Address32 populated), replacing aggregate axon event-only emission. This enables projection edge route construction under live streams.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

comments.author: Hailey Collet

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented definition-backed topology hydration path in Workbench Visualizer: on brain select/focus, it now requests ExportBrainDefinition (fallback BrainInfo.BaseDefinition), loads .nbn from artifact store, and seeds full region/route topology without waiting for stream activity. Added runtime support in HiveMind for ExportBrainDefinition/RequestSnapshot responses via IoGateway forwarding. Full-brain view can now render structural routes from definition data, and focused region mode can preload neuron/axon topology touching the focus region.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Crash hotfix: Workbench launch failure fixed by guarding VizActivityCanvasLayoutBuilder.BuildRegionEdges against empty aggregate sets. Startup path can now render region nodes with zero routes without throwing. Added regression test Build_DoesNotThrow_WhenRegionNodesExistWithoutAnyRoutes.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up interaction/topology pass: improved edge direction visibility (thicker direction border + dash line types), split hover handling to avoid canvas re-render flicker, added tap handlers for node/edge selection, and expanded definition artifact root discovery to include per-brain subdirectories so focus mode can hydrate neuron/axon topology beyond activity stream-only nodes.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Added definition-reference propagation for Visualizer topology hydration: BrainHost now accepts/registers nbn/nbs artifact refs (--nbn-sha256/--nbn-size/--artifact-store-uri), Designer and Orchestrator now pass those args when launching BrainHost, and IoGateway now serves ExportBrainDefinition/RequestSnapshot directly from its registered brain metadata (with HiveMind fallback + cache update). Added integration tests for IoGateway export/snapshot local metadata responses. Note: brains already running without BaseDefinition metadata still report source=none until re-registered or respawned.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Improved Visualizer interaction + stability: replaced per-item hover/click with canvas-level hit resolution, added persistent hover detail card near pointer (copyable text), widened edge hit-test stroke, and included hover summaries with event/tick values. Fixed UI lock/crash path by bounding visualizer event flush work per UI dispatch pass (MaxEventsPerUiFlush=256) instead of unbounded while-loop on UI thread. Added layout tests for dense focus-mode graphs and hit-test thickness assertions.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Added tick-aware render coalescing in VizPanel: streaming refresh now runs at most on cadence and only when a new tick is observed for the current selection (with forced render on queue drain), reducing dense-focus relayout pressure. Reduced per-dispatch stream batch size to 96 events. Also lowered local Workbench HiveMind launch defaults to --tick-hz 8 --min-tick-hz 2 as temporary safety baseline.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Addressed remaining high-CPU/freeze symptoms in focused region view: (1) focused edge renderer now collapses gateway fan-out routes by displayed endpoints (e.g., repeated N0->R8 merges into one visual edge), cutting edge count and hit-test load; (2) stream queue now drops oldest pending events above cap (1600) and schedules continuation at Dispatcher background priority; (3) streaming tick gating now uses focus-aware tick detection (events touching focus region only) so unrelated brain activity no longer forces full focused-canvas rebuilds.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Workbench integration now drives runtime viz scope (not just hub subscription): Shell updates active visualization brain + optional focus region from current Visualizer state, and WorkbenchClient sends SetBrainVisualization to HiveMind. This keeps behavior generic for any future client and aligns visual stream production with selected brain/view scope.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Also expanded artifact root probing (repo artifacts, demo paths, current/base directories) to improve definition discovery across launch modes.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Scope this issue to tooltip lifecycle and deterministic target precedence only.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Hover tooltip clears after leaving all targets/off-canvas (no target switch required), with bounded delay and no permanent persistence. 2) If pointer is inside a node circle, node must always win hover and click target resolution over edges. 3) Keep click usability forgiving, but hover hit path should not cling off-target. 4) Add/adjust regression tests for hover clear behavior and node-over-edge precedence; Release build/test passes.
close_reason: Completed implementation and regression coverage; Release tests pass.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Also updated NBNv2_Design.md Aleph reliability notes with explicit codex/codex.cmd preflight and blocked-sub-query fallback guidance discovered in this session. title: Visualizer layout follow-up: no-overlap guarantees and adaptive spacing
description: Address remaining layout clarity issues in full-brain and focus-region canvases: overlaps can still occur, dense regions need larger placement radii/separation, and edge radial spacing should increase when canvas room exists. Scope is layout/geometry only (separate from tooltip lifecycle).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Focus-region layout for dense neuron counts (>=40) increases placement radii and node spacing adaptively, with no node overlap at rendered diameters. 2) Whole-brain layout enforces non-overlap for region nodes under full 32-region population. 3) Edge radial spacing/arc separation expands when available room exists to improve readability without pushing nodes out of bounds. 4) Add/adjust regression tests for dense focus spacing, whole-brain overlap constraints, and edge separation behavior; Release build/test passes.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Added regression test Build_FocusModeDenseRegionUsesWiderRadialLayerSpacingWithoutForcingFullOnscreenFit plus full Release test gate pass (121/121).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up on reported 41-neuron overlap case: made focus gateway orbit dynamic and explicitly outside neuron orbit (gateway min orbit is now derived from neuronMaxOrbit + clearance), increased dense-neuron overflow scaling and ring-gap parameters, and disabled canvas clamping for gateway ring placement in focus mode. Final validation pass complete with user acceptance ("Everything is great").

title: Visualizer perf: move projection/layout build off UI thread
description: Run projection and layout synthesis on a background worker and publish immutable snapshots to UI; keep UI thread for lightweight apply only.
acceptance_criteria: Projection/layout computation no longer blocks UI input; pointer/click/scroll stay responsive during sustained event ingress.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in Release build/test gate.

title: Visualizer perf: incremental keyed diff updates for canvas collections
description: Stop full clear/rebuild of CanvasNodes/CanvasEdges on refresh; apply keyed add/update/remove deltas so unchanged items are retained.
acceptance_criteria: No full collection reset when graph topology is unchanged; reduced allocations and smoother frame-to-frame updates.
notes: Implemented incremental keyed diff updates for CanvasNodes/CanvasEdges in VizPanelViewModel (no full clear/rebuild on stable topology). Added regression test for keyed diff reordering/retention.
close_reason: Incremental keyed diff pipeline implemented and validated by Workbench tests.

title: Visualizer perf: spatial index + throttled hover hit-testing
description: Replace O(edge-count) pointer-move hit testing with broad-phase spatial lookup and bounded precise checks; throttle pointer processing cadence.
acceptance_criteria: Hover/select remains stable with dense graphs (>=500 edges) and pointer-move CPU spikes are eliminated.
notes: Implemented throttled pointer-move processing, spatial broad-phase hit index in VizPanelViewModel, and sticky hover near-miss tolerance; added interaction tests (VizPanelViewModelInteractionTests).
close_reason: Implemented and validated via Release build + tests

title: Visualizer perf: cache typed edge geometry instead of regenerating path strings
description: Avoid rebuilding path-data strings every refresh; cache/reuse typed bezier geometry keyed by route endpoints/layout version.
acceptance_criteria: Edge geometry generation cost drops significantly on steady topology; repeated frames reuse cached geometry artifacts.
notes: Implemented edge-curve cache keyed by source/target/control topology to reuse precomputed Bezier control points and path strings across frames. Added deterministic reuse assertion in VizActivityCanvasLayoutBuilderTests to verify repeated builds share cached path artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in Release build/test gate.

title: Runtime perf: skip viz aggregation in RegionShard CPU backend when viz is off/scope-filtered
description: Bypass axon/fired visualization aggregation work in RegionShardCpuBackend when brain visualization is disabled or route is out of current focus scope.
acceptance_criteria: With visualization disabled for a brain, compute path performs no viz aggregation allocations and tick throughput is unaffected by viz plumbing.
notes: Added RegionShardVisualizationComputeScope to CPU backend and actor wiring so viz aggregation/allocation is skipped when disabled and reduced when focus-scoped; added RegionShardCpuBackendVisualizationTests.
close_reason: Implemented and validated via Release build + tests

title: Visualizer perf instrumentation: frame/layout/hit-test timing + queue depth diagnostics
description: Add measurable diagnostics for render frame time, projection time, layout time, hit-test time, queue depth, and drop counts to support performance tuning.
acceptance_criteria: Copy diagnostics includes new perf metrics and allows before/after comparison for each optimization issue.
notes: Added visualizer perf diagnostics for projection/layout/apply/frame timings, hit-test timing (last/avg/max/samples), queue depth/peak/drop counts, flush timing, and canvas diff stats. Included diagnostics coverage test.
close_reason: Perf instrumentation and diagnostics output landed with test coverage.

title: Visualizer perf: adaptive LOD policy for dense focus-region graphs
description: Introduce deterministic LOD controls (sampling/culling/aggregation thresholds) by zoom and edge density to keep large regions responsive while preserving signal semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Dense region views degrade gracefully under configurable budgets without lockups, and users can tune/disable LOD for fidelity testing.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in Release build/test gate.

title: Visualizer interaction: add performant pan/zoom viewport controls
description: Add optional 2D viewport controls for the Visualizer canvas (zoom + pan + reset) while preserving current interaction fidelity and responsiveness.
acceptance_criteria: Canvas supports zoom in/out with bounded scale and a reset-to-fit action; Canvas supports panning when zoomed without breaking node/edge hit-testing; Hover/selection tooltips stay anchored to the correct logical target under transform; Perf diagnostics remain within current budget for representative dense scenes; dotnet build/test Release with disabled build servers remains green.
close_reason: Completed implementation and verification; acceptance criteria met with release tests passing.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented viewport controls in Workbench visualizer: bounded zoom (+/- buttons, Ctrl+Wheel), fit-to-view action/button, pan via L/R/U/D buttons, Shift+drag and middle-drag, Shift+Wheel horizontal pan, and two-finger touch pinch/drag. Added double-click empty canvas => fit; double-click region/gateway => focus that region then fit; neuron double-click remains normal select. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Post-close regression fix: restored reliable canvas interaction while keeping zoom controls by switching pointer coordinate mapping back to ActivityCanvasSurface-local coordinates (instead of extent-host divided scaling), adding transparent background on ActivityCanvasSurface so empty-space pointer presses/double-clicks are captured, and making pan/fit offset clamping use explicit scaled canvas dimensions rather than stale ScrollViewer extent values. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up on viewport regressions: normalized canvas bounds from actual node/edge geometry (including overflow focus layouts), shifted layouts into positive space, and published dynamic ActivityCanvasWidth/Height so scroll extents match visible content. VizPanel now listens for canvas dimension property changes and re-syncs/clamps scroll offset on extent changes. This restores fit/pan/zoom behavior with off-canvas overflow while preserving hover/select hit testing. Verified via dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Additional follow-up on user validation: fixed zoom anchor drift by preserving viewport world-center with pan-aware math; enabled panning regardless of overflow by combining scroll-offset movement with translation fallback; accepted unmodified wheel (while focused on canvas) for zoom to improve touchpad behavior; removed static 860x420 edge-point clamping in BuildEdgeCurve so focus gateway/region edges connect at actual node positions even when nodes are outside baseline canvas. Also added persistent navigation padding around normalized canvas bounds. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: User-reported follow-up fixed: shift-drag panning direction flipped to expected behavior; wheel/touchpad zoom now requires Shift (ctrl-only wheel events are consumed to avoid accidental zoom while scrolling); fit centering reworked using symmetric content bounds (nodes+edges) so fit no longer throws graph out of view. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up fix for viewport regressions: restored Shift-drag pan direction; improved zoom anchor stability by absorbing clamped offset residual into canvas translation; made fit-to-view run a render-settled second pass to avoid stale-dimension misfits after view switches; kept wheel/touchpad zoom gated behind Shift with ctrl-only wheel consumed. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up UX pass: consolidated Designer spawn/validation into a single two-column card; blocked non-Shift wheel/touchpad movement over viz canvas; flipped Shift/middle drag panning direction per latest user expectation; replaced fit/default-view handling with deferred canvas-view application so fit/default centering no longer depends on stale viewport/layout state; and aligned focus gateway angular placement to whole-brain relative direction around the focused region. Also bound edge template canvas to dynamic ActivityCanvasWidth/Height to avoid fixed-size clipping. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Post-validation follow-up: removed automatic recenter hook on VisualizationSelectionChanged (it was firing during normal refresh and causing frequent unsolicited reset to center/default zoom); recenter now only triggers on explicit navigation-context changes (brain/focus changes) and explicit fit action. Also stabilized normalized canvas dimensions with jitter tolerance so activity pulse radius changes do not continuously resize the canvas/card. Shift-drag pan direction flipped back to expected behavior, and non-Shift wheel over canvas remains blocked. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up for persistent reset/scrollbar: removed navigation recenter noise by comparing true navigation identity (BrainId + FocusRegionId) before requesting default-center, so periodic SelectedBrain status updates no longer trigger resets. Wheel handling now runs in tunnel phase and short-circuits if already handled to prevent ScrollViewer from consuming non-Shift touchpad gestures first. Also hid internal canvas scrollbars (horizontal/vertical Hidden) to avoid internal bars entirely. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: UX follow-up: corrected Shift-drag direction again (drag direction now matches expected), changed canvas wheel behavior so only Shift+wheel is intercepted for zoom while non-Shift wheel is left unhandled to scroll the surrounding page, tightened canvas chrome (controls+hint row spacing, replaced read-only TextBox legend/selection/pinned lines with TextBlock, moved zoom label next to +/-), and capped canvas viewport height dynamically to visible top-level space so canvas remains in-view without requiring page scroll to see it. Internal canvas scrollbars are disabled. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Another follow-up after user repro: recenter trigger narrowed further to focus-region changes only (removed SelectedBrain from auto-center triggers so streaming brain-list churn cannot reset view), and fit/default-center now uses actual node/edge content bounds (plus small padding) instead of full padded canvas size, which corrects over-zoomed and off-center fit behavior. Shift-drag direction flipped again to match latest user expectation. Verified with dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (122/122).

title: Expand quantization/packing/invariant test coverage
description: Add Nbn.Tests coverage for quantization encode/decode edge cases, Address32/ShardId32 boundaries, neuron/axon packing limits, and invariant validation (input/output rules, sorted/duplicate axons).
acceptance_criteria: - Tests cover map types (linear/gamma signed/unsigned), center code mapping, and clamp behavior\n- Tests cover record boundaries (max codes, invalid values)\n- Invariant checks for IO restrictions and duplicate/sorted axon lists are exercised

title: Add runtime output subscriber backpressure for IO OOM protection
description: Follow-up after the Basics OOM where Nbn.Runtime.IO reached ~87 GiB RSS and was killed by the kernel. This pass bounded Basics client queues and cleaned pending IO subscriber maps on brain removal, but runtime output fan-out still uses fire-and-forget Send to subscribers with no subscriber-level backpressure/dead-subscriber pruning. Investigate runtime-level output subscriber flow control, stale subscriber cleanup, or latest-only/dropping subscription modes so a slow/disconnected external client cannot grow IO outbound memory without bound.
acceptance_criteria: 1. IO output fan-out has a bounded-memory behavior for slow or disconnected subscribers. 2. Existing exact-output subscription semantics are either preserved by default or documented if an opt-in dropping/latest-only mode is introduced. 3. Regression coverage simulates a slow/dead subscriber without unbounded memory growth.
close_reason: Implemented opt-in latest-only IO output delivery, subscriber termination cleanup, telemetry, docs, and regression coverage. Full runtime suite passed after rerun: 1177/1177.

title: Refactor IoGatewayActor service-forwarding partials
description: Extract IoGatewayActor reproduction/speciation forwarding and failure-shaping logic into a dedicated partial to reduce main-file size and keep gateway orchestration focused, without changing IO contracts or runtime behavior.
acceptance_criteria: IoGatewayActor service-forwarding logic lives outside the main file; build and focused/full tests pass without behavior changes.
close_reason: Completed IoGatewayActor service-forwarding partial extraction with green focused and full-suite validation.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Completed by extracting IoGatewayActor reproduction/speciation forwarding plus failure-response shaping into IoGatewayActor.ReproSpeciation.cs. Validation: dotnet build src/Nbn.Runtime.IO/Nbn.Runtime.IO.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers; dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp/io-final --filter FullyQualifiedName~IoGatewayArtifactReferenceTests; dotnet build NBNv2.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp/finalbuild; dotnet test NBNv2.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp/finaltest.

title: Capture live speciation timeout/perf trace after bootstrap root-sample fix
description: Run a fresh Workbench/EvolutionSim capture after the root-sample bootstrap fix. Verify whether newborn derived species now accumulate compatibility-backed root samples with healthy split proximity, and if commit timeouts still occur collect the exact speciation/reproduction timing path plus request counts for the slow branch.
close_reason: Resolved by the landed runtime/bootstrap fixes plus the final Workbench bounded-history follow-up; remaining live concerns are tracked separately in NBNv2-3oe and NBNv2-98p.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up created after landing commit 0b43bf4. Next live rerun should verify that newborn derived species accumulate compatibility-backed root samples, split proximity starts materially above zero, and speciation commit timeouts no longer occur under EvolutionSim.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Landed follow-up runtime fix: bootstrap now rebuilds recent-derived target hints from the persisted founder row when same-species continuity enters a newborn derived species, and founder hindsight reassignment now snapshots the newborn species' earliest exemplars once per split so the batch cannot recursively expand compatibility fan-out. Validation passed locally (SpeciationManagerActorTests 39/39, full suite 708/708, docs render/check). Next live capture should verify that early derived memberships now record assigned_species_similarity_source=compatibility_assessment with materially positive split proximity, and that decision gaps around founder/seed commits fall below the prior ~10s spikes.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Fresh live capture diagnosis: founder rows are now correct (assigned split proximity +0.14 on decisions 41 and 87), but derived species still strand at one member because bootstrap admissions are timing out in the reproduction mailbox. Live DB shows 53 lineage_bootstrap_compatibility_required fallbacks and the top decision gaps are all those rows at ~5.1s-7.3s. Root cause was ReproductionManagerActor awaiting each request inline, so speciation assessment requests sat behind unrelated reproduction/brain-resolution work. Landed fix: reproduction requests now respond via ReenterAfter, and added a regression that proves an artifact compatibility assessment can complete while a separate brain-id resolution request is still blocked on IO. Validation passed locally (ReproductionManagerActorTests+SpeciationManagerActorTests 80/80, full suite 709/709, docs render/check). Next live rerun should verify derived species gain non-founder members instead of repeated bootstrap fallbacks and that the ~5s stalls disappear.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Fresh live trace showed two gaps the prior fix missed: founder commits were still blocking on bounded hindsight compatibility work (up to ~15s total), and speciation was discarding valid manual similarity scores whenever reproduction returned SimilarityReport.compatible=false. Landed follow-up runtime fix: founder hindsight now runs as queued post-commit work instead of blocking the originating split assignment; speciation now uses finite compatibility-assessment similarity scores unless the abort reason is an infrastructure/load failure; reproduction caches parsed artifact parents by SHA for repeated assessment-only requests. Validation passed locally (SpeciationManagerActorTests+ReproductionManagerActorTests 83/83, full suite 712/712, docs render/check). Next live rerun should verify founder decision gaps collapse, newborn species no longer strand at one member from bootstrap timeouts, and metadata records assigned_species_compatibility_report_compatible/abort_reason when an incompatible manual score is still used for admission.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Reviewed the latest live capture before editing. Every lineage_bootstrap_compatibility_required row in that DB took >=5000 ms (44/44; median 5014 ms; max 9049 ms), and all of those rows were ArtifactRef candidates with blank assigned_species_compatibility provenance. That rules out the quick low-score fallback path for this capture: bootstrap admission is hitting the null/timeout branch, not a fast score-rejected branch. Landed runtime follow-up: speciation now preserves assigned-species compatibility attempt provenance even on fallback (attempted/admitted booleans, failure_reason, elapsed_ms, abort_reason, score when present, exemplar IDs), and added regressions for low-score fallback provenance plus explicit compatibility-request timeout fallback provenance. Validation passed locally: SpeciationManagerActorTests 42/42, full suite 713/713, docs render/check. Next live rerun under NBNv2-5vg should verify whether fallback rows now show compatibility_request_timeout versus score-based rejection and whether the reproduction-side latency still exceeds the 5 s speciation budget.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Fresh live rerun still reproduced the singleton/slowness artifact after 18847a2, but the new fallback provenance made the branch unambiguous: every newborn bootstrap fallback recorded assigned_species_compatibility_attempted=true with assigned_species_compatibility_failure_reason=compatibility_request_timeout, assigned_species_compatibility_abort_reason=repro_request_timeout, and elapsed_ms ~5.0s. That ruled out a fast low-score rejection and pointed to speciation's direct remoting call into reproduction. Root cause was transport config drift: SpeciationRemote.BuildConfig registered NbnCommon/NbnSpeciation/NbnSettings descriptors but omitted NbnReproReflection, so speciation-originated AssessCompatibilityByArtifactsRequest traffic could not marshal across Proto.Actor remoting even though the same reproduction assessment APIs still worked via IO. Landed fix: SpeciationRemote now registers NbnReproReflection, and added a cross-node integration regression proving a sender using SpeciationRemote can round-trip Repro.AssessCompatibilityByArtifactsRequest to a real ReproductionManagerActor and receive Repro.ReproduceResult. Validation passed locally: targeted RemoteDescriptorTransportTests+SpeciationManagerActorTests 46/46, full suite 714/714, docs freshness check passed. Keep 5vg open for one more live rerun to confirm newborn species now accumulate non-founder members and the ~5s stalls disappear.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Fresh live rerun after c8f55ca shows the perf half is fixed but the semantic half remains: speciation decision gaps collapsed (overall p95 ~259 ms, max 1726 ms), yet newborn derived species still strand at one member and early split proximity stays low because bootstrap admission now fails fast instead of timing out. Live DB has 564 lineage_bootstrap_compatibility_required rows, and sampled fallbacks show assigned_species_compatibility_attempted=true with finite scores around 0.47-0.49, assigned_species_compatibility_failure_reason=compatibility_similarity_below_threshold, and assigned_species_compatibility_abort_reason=repro_function_hist_mismatch; founders are still healthy at +0.14 assigned split proximity. Root cause was config drift inside SpeciationManagerActor: CreateCompatibilityAssessmentConfig only set SpawnChildNever, so protobuf defaults drove reproduction assessment with MaxRegionSpanDiffRatio/MaxFunctionHistDistance/MaxConnectivityHistDistance effectively at 0 instead of the active reproduction thresholds. Landed fix: speciation now caches compatibility-assessment config from reproduction settings/defaults with SpawnChild forced to Never and clones that config into every bootstrap compatibility request. Added regression proving newborn bootstrap compatibility requests honor reproduction settings thresholds while disabling spawn, and updated the runtime spec note accordingly. Validation passed locally: SpeciationManagerActorTests 43/43, full suite 715/715, docs render/check passed. Keep 5vg open for another live rerun to verify newborn species now admit non-founder members and split proximity no longer collapses after founder rows.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Reviewed the latest live rerun before this round of edits. Fresh DB had 20 species / 3726 decisions / 2455 bootstrap fallbacks; decision-gap median was 5 ms, p95 ~1148 ms, max 16678 ms, so the old ~5 s timeout wall is gone and there is no hard stop at 2048 fetched or 20 species (EvolutionSim status kept advancing speciation_commit_successes past 3700 while parent_pool_size stayed at the configured max 512). The remaining runtime pattern was semantic: every bootstrap fallback was fast and score-based (compatibility_similarity_below_threshold), with finite assigned-species compatibility scores clustered below the old 0.92 match threshold. Landed follow-up fixes: (1) newborn bootstrap admission now uses the assigned species dynamic split threshold and allows near-ties against the source species within the configured split-guard margin instead of forcing the full lineage match threshold; (2) Workbench split-proximity parsing now prefers assigned/generic split-proximity metadata over source-species proximity so operator-facing charts stop reporting newborn members from the wrong perspective; (3) EvolutionSim parent-pool turnover is now diversity-aware at capacity, adding successful committed children only when they displace the most overrepresented evictable species and skipping additions that would crowd out a rarer species already represented in the pool. Validation passed locally: targeted SpeciationManagerActorTests + EvolutionSimulationSessionTests + SpeciationPanelViewModelTests 91/91, full suite 717/717, docs render/check passed. Next live rerun should tell us whether the remaining singleton/plateau behavior collapses once the softer bootstrap gate and diversity-preserving pool retention are both in place.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Reviewed the latest live rerun before this round of edits. Fresh DB still had memberships/decisions growing well past the Workbench freeze point (4712 memberships / 4722 decisions / 74 species), so the apparent stop at fetched=2048 was a history paging bug in the Workbench/API path rather than a runtime commit halt. Bootstrap fallbacks were no longer timeout-dominated; they were mostly fast score rejections, and provenance showed assigned_species_compatibility_exemplar_count split across 1/2/3 exemplars (299/143/408). That pointed at the remaining 2-3 member stall: bootstrap admission still took the min score across up to three early exemplars, making the gate harsher as a newborn species tried to leave the bootstrap window. Landed follow-up fixes: (1) SpeciationListHistory now supports offset paging end-to-end (proto/store/actor/client), and Workbench now pages through full history instead of freezing charts/status at the first 2048-row slice; (2) bootstrap admission now evaluates against one fixed earliest exemplar during the newborn bootstrap window instead of ratcheting to the min across every early member; (3) EvolutionSim parent selection bias now normalizes by current species representation in non-neutral modes so dominant species do not monopolize parent picks simply by occupying more pool slots. Added regressions for list-history paging, Workbench multi-page history loading, bootstrap third-member single-exemplar admission, proto field stability, and representation-normalized parent selection. Validation passed locally: targeted tests 119/119, full suite 720/720, docs render/check passed. Keep 5vg open for the next live rerun to confirm that the 2048 UI freeze is gone, 2-3 member stalls collapse, and species plateaus are reduced under the normalized parent-selection path.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Latest live rerun indicates the original timeout/bootstrap singleton/fetched=2048 freeze cluster is resolved: newborn species now grow, initial low similarity was fixed, memberships continue past the prior freeze point, and this pass makes the visible chart window the real bounded recent-history fetch window while shortening derived-species labels. Remaining concerns have been split into NBNv2-3oe (deep single-branch lineage splitting) and NBNv2-98p (Workbench responsiveness with large taxonomies).

title: Wire InputCoordinator drain into tick delivery
description: Input writes are currently buffered in BrainSignalRouter and forwarded to input shards during TickDeliver. InputCoordinator in IO is still unused. Wire a proper drain path so IO handles buffering per spec and router just delivers drained contributions.
close_reason: Completed

title: Reproduction config parity: add missing protocol knobs for inbound-reroute distance and per-region out-degree
description: Design section 14 references configurable inbound reroute max-distance and optional per-region average out-degree caps, but nbn_repro.proto currently exposes only prob_reroute_inbound_axon_on_delete and max_avg_out_degree_brain. Add protocol fields, runtime handling, and Workbench controls for full parity.
close_reason: Implemented proto/runtime/workbench parity for inbound reroute max-distance and per-region out-degree caps

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed protocol parity for reproduction config: added proto fields for inbound reroute max ring distance and per-region out-degree caps, wired runtime enforcement in ReproductionManagerActor, added Workbench controls/mapping, and added proto/runtime/workbench tests. Verified with dotnet build/test -c Release --disable-build-servers.

title: Investigate post-split newborn-species similarity floor semantics
description: Observed in live EvolutionSim+Speciation runs: new derived species frequently appear with lineage similarity very close to the effective split threshold, then rapidly plateau above threshold. Need runtime-level analysis of whether this is expected from current lineage similarity definition (child-vs-dominant parent), assignment policy semantics, or species boundary definition.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Quantify first-split similarity/proximity distribution for derived species across runs (including per-species trajectories after split).
2) Document whether current similarity metric semantics intrinsically bias newborn species toward threshold-adjacent values.
3) Propose and evaluate at least two runtime alternatives (e.g., species-centroid/cluster distance, temporal divergence accumulation, parent-set distance composition).
4) Add instrumentation and tests needed to compare current and candidate semantics deterministically.
5) Update speciation/runtime docs with chosen behavior and operator interpretation guidance.
close_reason: Completed runtime floor/source semantics fix, added collision regression coverage, and created follow-up NBNv2-8di for fresh live capture validation.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Initial quantified signal from latest speciation.db run: split_species_count=10; first_split_similarity range=0.831904..0.839991 at effective_split_threshold=0.84; first_split_proximity range=-0.008096..-0.000009 (mean~-0.00425). Post-split samples per species often rebound to ~0.86-0.89 quickly. This supports a semantics-level investigation (not just threshold tuning).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Additional quantified evidence under requested policy profile (match=0.90, split=0.86, guard=0.025, effective split=0.835, min-parent=1, consensus=0.70): latest decision sample still shows newborn diverged species created near threshold. Observed split proximity (lineage_similarity - effective_split): min=-0.00825, max=-0.00069, mean=-0.00382 across recent divergence events. Non-diverged similarity mean remains ~0.8805. This strengthens hypothesis that similarity semantics/species-boundary definition, not only thresholds, drive immediate post-split near-threshold behavior.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
﻿Implemented NBNv2-6mp with runtime-first semantics (not test-only):

Runtime changes:
- Speciation assignment now applies a dynamic effective split threshold:
  max(policy_effective_split_threshold, species_floor_similarity - lineage_hysteresis_margin).
- species_floor_similarity is persisted/rebuilt as the minimum full-consensus intra-species lineage similarity sample for each species (sample only when assigned species == dominant species and dominant_share == 1.0).
- New derived species do not seed their own floor from the divergence event; floor forms from subsequent in-species assignments.
- Decision metadata now records policy/dynamic thresholds, threshold source, species-floor stats, and split proximity deltas for deterministic analysis.
- Startup now primes floor state from persisted membership history; reset/new-epoch paths clear the floor cache.

Operator surface:
- Workbench split-proximity parsing now prefers assignment_policy.lineage_dynamic_split_threshold when present, falling back to split-threshold-minus-guard for older metadata.

Regression coverage added/updated:
- Dynamic floor causes near-threshold split when intra-species floor is higher.
- Dynamic floor persists/rebuilds across restart from speciation.db history.
- Realign ordering remains intact when dynamic floor is present (realign path still wins before split branch).
- Workbench split-proximity chart/test now validates dynamic threshold consumption.

Validation:
- dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Speciation.SpeciationManagerActorTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Workbench.SpeciationPanelViewModelTests" (46/46)
- dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (679/679)
- docs render + freshness check passed.

Docs updated:
- src/Nbn.Runtime.Speciation/Design.md
- tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/Design.md
- docs/sections/protocols/19-protocol-schemas-proto.md
- docs/NBNv2.md regenerated.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed runtime-first fix with intra-species semantics and bounded hindsight support. Core changes: (1) assignment now prefers dominant-species pairwise similarity evidence (parent_a/parent_b) before fallback similarity; (2) species floor sampling now records only intra-species pairwise estimates via lineage.intra_species_similarity_sample (+ species id), avoiding cross-species aggregate contamination; (3) split proximity metadata now reflects assignment similarity, including lineage_assignment_similarity_score and dominant_species_similarity_score fields; (4) bounded hindsight reassignment remains supplemental and now avoids carrying forward stale intra-species sample fields on reassigned memberships; (5) settings/profile wiring fixed for lineage_hindsight_reassign_commit_window and lineage_hindsight_similarity_margin (keys added to shared settings + defaults, snapshot builder no longer hardcodes values). Added tests: mixed-parent pairwise assignment/floor behavior, hindsight recent-source reassignment window+margin path, store reassignment/list-recent APIs, settings defaults/runtime-config key coverage. Validation: dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp passed (683/683). Docs updated in speciation/runtime/workbench/protocol docs and docs/NBNv2.md regenerated.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Workbench follow-up fixed: Speciation pane now exposes hindsight window and hindsight margin, and Workbench load/apply/persist tests cover the new speciation settings keys. Validation: dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (685 passed); docs render/check passed.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up runtime fix for persistent newborn-species fragmentation: (1) hindsight/source-species reassignment now uses a symmetric founder-similarity band (founder +/- lineage_hindsight_similarity_margin) instead of an upper-only bound, preventing much more divergent members from being pulled into the newborn species; (2) runtime now reuses a recent derived species for nearby post-split source members from the same source species, preventing sibling branches for the same emerging cluster. Added regressions for both behaviors and updated runtime/protocol docs. Validation: targeted speciation slice 25/25, targeted speciation+Workbench slice 51/51, full suite 686/686, docs render/check passed.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up runtime fix for persistent newborn resplits: recent founder-band clustering was not enough; newborn derived species could still become dominant and split again before recording any in-species similarity sample. Added runtime guard so a newborn derived species cannot split again until it has at least one in-species sample, plus regressions for (1) no immediate resplit and (2) nested split becomes allowed after the first in-species sample lands. Validation: speciation slice 27/27, full suite 688/688, docs render/check passed.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: User reported low newborn intra-species similarity persisted; revisiting similarity semantics and early mixed-parent assignment.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up completion for the remaining semantics bug: runtime now separates source/dominant split similarity from assigned-species/intra-species similarity instead of conflating them. Core changes: (1) split founders seed lineage.intra_species_similarity_sample=1.0 and lineage_assignment_similarity_score=1.0 for the newborn species, while source/dominant split similarity remains recorded separately; (2) low-consensus mixed-parent assignments can now rescue into the strongest existing parent species when parent-specific pairwise similarity meets lineage_match_threshold, preventing nearby early descendants from defaulting away from a newborn species they strongly match; (3) Workbench now uses assigned-species similarity for the current-epoch within-species divergence label and explicit/source split-proximity metadata for split-pressure charts. Added regressions for singleton founder seeding, immediate resplit when a follow-on member is not similar enough, low-consensus pairwise rescue, assigned-species divergence parsing, and explicit split-proximity parsing. Validation: targeted Speciation+Workbench slice passed (56/56), full dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp passed (691/691), docs render + freshness check passed. Follow-up created for EvolutionSim sim_commit telemetry alignment: NBNv2-h87.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: User reports live behavior still unchanged after semantic split fix; investigating real run data and remaining runtime path.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed the source-species runtime fix. The resolver no longer anchors split/assignment on a dominant/count-recency winner when parent-specific pairwise evidence exists: it now carries an explicit source species chosen from the best parent-species pairwise fit, uses that source for split thresholding, derived-species rooting, hindsight/reuse, and metadata, and only falls back when lineage is otherwise unambiguous. Newborn derived species still seed intra-species similarity at 1.0, but the live-failure regression is now covered by a mixed-parent split test where recency would have picked the wrong parent species and the derived species must root from the best-fit source species instead. Also tightened hysteresis and metadata tests around source_species_* semantics. Validation: SpeciationManagerActorTests 29/29, full dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp 692/692, docs render/check passed.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed the remaining runtime fix. Confirmed from preserved investigate_6mp runs that derived species were re-splitting before any non-founder in-species sample landed, which meant the founder's synthetic 1.0 was incorrectly satisfying the newborn evidence gate. Runtime changes: (1) founder singleton similarity remains in metadata but no longer seeds the dynamic species floor or newborn re-split guard; (2) when runtime keeps continuity with an assigned species different from the resolved source species, it now carries provisional assigned-species similarity/intra-species sample evidence from the resolved source similarity so newborn species floors switch to the assigned species sooner; (3) generic split-threshold/proximity metadata now reflects the assigned-species view, while explicit source-prefixed threshold/proximity fields preserve source-lineage split pressure for operator tooling; (4) BuildDerivedSpeciesId now preserves the hashed suffix under the 96-char cap to prevent deep-branch ID collisions. Added regressions for realign/reuse assigned-similarity carryover, founder-singleton guard behavior, split-after-first-non-founder sample, and long-ID uniqueness. Validation: targeted speciation/workbench slice 65/65, full dotnet test 700/700, docs render/check passed. Follow-up created for fresh live capture validation: NBNv2-8di.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Investigated the Demo integration timeout and found the test helper itself was too brittle: WaitForBrainInfo spent the entire outer timeout budget on each individual BrainInfo request, so one slow metadata/bootstrap response could fail the whole test before the polling loop had a chance to retry. Stabilized the helper to use short per-request timeouts with retry inside the overall deadline, which matches the IO design expectation that BrainInfo metadata bootstrap can lag briefly. Validation against NBNv2-6mp follow-up conditions: (1) founder singleton similarity no longer seeds the species floor: founder decisions still record intra_species_similarity_sample=1.0, but no pre-actual-sample species-floor row depended on singleton/1.0 evidence; every early species-floor use carried non-1.0 assigned-species similarity evidence, and founder-singleton-like floor rows = 0. (2) recent derived continuity paths no longer lose assigned-species samples: among 310 derived assignments where assigned species differed from resolved source species, 0 rows were missing lineage_assignment_similarity_score / intra_species_similarity_sample / intra_species_similarity_species_id==assigned species. (3) derived-species ID cap path hit the 96-character limit in live data (max species_id length = 96) without collision-like reuse across different source species or lineage keys; one repeated 96-char species ID was only reused for the same source species + same lineage key, consistent with same-lineage reuse rather than a truncation collision.

Note on the original AppData Workbench speciation.db: by the time this pass inspected it, it had already been reset to an empty current epoch (taxonomy_config_snapshots/taxonomy_epochs only), so I used a fresh isolated capture rather than treating the cleared DB as runtime evidence.

title: Implement mixed-topology multi-endpoint discovery and routing
description: Goal: make distributed NBN deployments end-user transparent across mixed Tailscale/LAN/public-IP topologies. An operator should only need to connect to a reachable SettingsMonitor address; after that, Workbench and runtime peers should discover multiple endpoint candidates per node/service/callback target and automatically choose a mutually reachable route for each caller/target pair. Scope includes protocol/schema, SettingsMonitor persistence/discovery, shared endpoint resolution, Workbench callback flows, and runtime control-plane consumers that currently rely on a single raw host:port/actor or PID string. Acceptance clues: 1) services and callback publishers can register repeated endpoint candidates with stable logical identities, 2) callers resolve best-fit routes per target without manual per-service advertise-host coordination, 3) mixed deployments where one peer reaches a node via Tailscale and another reaches the same node via LAN/public address work as long as each pair has at least one mutually reachable candidate, 4) legacy single-endpoint publishers/consumers remain compatible during migration, 5) docs/spec reflect the new endpoint-resolution model and remaining constraints. Initial implementation plan: phase 1 add shared candidate models + proto/schema support + discovery encoding/decoding with backward-compatible single-endpoint fallback; phase 2 add caller-side shared resolver with probe/cache selection; phase 3 migrate Workbench discovery/reconcile/callback flows; phase 4 migrate runtime control-plane and subscriber/callback paths; phase 5 remove remaining single-address assumptions after compatibility window. Risks: callback flows currently embed raw subscriber PIDs, placement/runtime surfaces embed single addresses, and Proto.Actor transport expects one concrete PID at send time, so migration must separate logical target identity from resolved transport address. Constraint: transparency cannot bypass missing network reachability; every communicating pair still needs at least one mutually reachable candidate.
close_reason: Core mixed-topology discovery, placement, callback, and runtime routing loops are implemented and verified; remaining residue is tracked separately in NBNv2-4id.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn: landed the shared discovery groundwork in Nbn.Shared. Added ServiceEndpointCandidateKind, ServiceEndpointCandidate, ServiceEndpointSet, ServiceEndpointSetRegistration, endpoint-set key helpers, JSON encode/decode for repeated endpoint candidates, and ServiceEndpointDiscoveryClient publish/resolve helpers for endpoint sets with backward-compatible fallback to the existing single service.endpoint.* keys. Added focused tests covering round-trip parsing, publish-set legacy fallback publication, and resolve-set fallback from legacy single endpoints. Full suite passed (1007 tests). Remaining work: integrate endpoint-set persistence/consumption across SettingsMonitor/runtime publishers, add caller-side route resolver/probing, migrate Workbench callback/reconcile flows, then migrate runtime control-plane payloads and subscriber registration paths that still embed raw PID/address strings.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 2: runtime publishers now emit endpoint-set registrations in addition to the legacy single service.endpoint.* keys. Added NetworkAddressDefaults endpoint-set/candidate generation and static TryPublishSetAsync helper, then switched HiveMind, IO, WorkerNode, Reproduction, Speciation, and Observability startup publication to publish multi-candidate endpoint sets with legacy fallback. The candidate builder marks the current advertised endpoint as default and also includes additional discovered LAN/tunnel/public interface candidates while filtering obvious container bridge interfaces. Added a focused NetworkAddressDefaults test and updated the distributed-architecture doc section to note service.endpoint_set.* publication. Full suite passed (1008 tests). Remaining work is still on the consumer side: caller-side route resolution/probing, Workbench discovery/reconcile/callback migration, and runtime control-plane/subscriber flows that still consume only single addresses or raw PID strings.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 3: added shared ServiceEndpointResolver probe-based candidate selection and migrated Workbench Orchestrator refresh to prefer service.endpoint_set.* registrations over legacy single endpoints. Orchestrator now resolves service endpoints per key by probing candidate host:port reachability and selecting the first reachable candidate in priority/default order, then falls back to the preferred candidate if none probe successfully. Added focused shared resolver tests plus an Orchestrator test proving endpoint-set selection overrides an unreachable legacy endpoint. Full suite passed (1011 tests); one DemoIntegration test failed once during the first full-suite run but passed immediately in isolation and the subsequent full-suite rerun was fully green, so no deterministic regression was left unresolved. Remaining work: migrate more Workbench/runtime consumers beyond Orchestrator refresh, especially placement reconcile/hosted-actor/node-address paths and callback/subscriber registrations that still rely on raw single addresses or PID strings.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 4: added node-specific endpoint-set publication and consumption. Introduced NodeEndpointSetSettings key parsing/building plus SettingsMonitorReporter publication of node.endpoint_set.<node-id> settings using the same multi-candidate endpoint-set payload shape. Runtime reporters now publish node endpoint sets for service roots and remoted hosts, and Workbench Orchestrator hosted-actor / placement-reconcile refresh now resolves node contacts through those endpoint sets instead of trusting only NodeStatus.Address. Added shared node-endpoint-set tests plus an Orchestrator test proving placement reconcile uses a resolved node endpoint-set address rather than the raw node address. Full suite passed (1014 tests). Remaining work: broader runtime-side consumption of node/service endpoint sets beyond Workbench, especially callback/subscriber registration payloads and control-plane/runtime surfaces that still carry raw single-address PID strings.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 5: made the shared ServiceEndpointDiscoveryClient endpoint-set aware so runtime consumers can benefit automatically. ResolveAsync/ResolveKnownAsync now prefer service.endpoint_set.* registrations, BuildKeyFilter watches both legacy and endpoint-set keys, and live SettingChanged processing resolves endpoint-set updates back into legacy-shaped ServiceEndpointRegistration observations for existing consumers. Added deterministic tests for endpoint-set preference and subscription updates in ServiceEndpointDiscoveryClientTests. Full suite passed (1016 tests). Remaining work is now concentrated in the hardest area: callback/subscriber/control-plane payloads that still embed raw PID strings or single addresses instead of logical target ids plus caller-side resolution.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 6: introduced a shared RoutablePidReference format for callback/subscriber fields that currently only carry raw PID labels. DebugHubActor, VizHubActor, SettingsMonitorActor, and OutputCoordinatorActor now accept encoded multi-candidate subscriber references and resolve them to a concrete PID via caller-side endpoint-set selection before storing the subscription. Workbench now generates and reuses a routable subscriber reference for its receiver actor, uses it for Settings/Debug/Viz subscriptions and visualization subscriber fields, and forwards it through WorkbenchReceiverActor for IO output subscriptions. Added focused tests for RoutablePidReference plus encoded-subscriber delivery in DebugHubActorTests and OutputCoordinatorActorTests. Full suite passed (1020 tests). Remaining work is still on deeper control-plane/runtime surfaces that embed raw PID strings or addresses outside these subscription flows, such as additional runtime callback registrations and placement/runtime payloads that still assume one concrete transport route.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 7: extended callback/control-plane support by introducing async routable-subscriber resolution in additional runtime consumers. HiveMind visualization subscriber resolution now accepts encoded routable subscriber refs, and DebugHub/VizHub/SettingsMonitor/OutputCoordinator subscriber handling plus Workbench subscriber publication were landed and verified together. Added focused tests for encoded callback refs and reran HiveMind/Observability/IO/shared discovery coverage; full suite passed (1020 tests). Remaining work is now narrower but still non-trivial: deeper runtime/control-plane fields that are not subscriber references yet still embed raw PID/address strings (for example selected placement/runtime metadata and cross-service actor references such as router/output/shard pid strings) need migration to routable references or logical-target resolution.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 8: carried multi-endpoint routing into runtime control-plane actor references. Added shared RoutablePidReference actor-set builders, preserved actor-reference strings in HiveMind brain state, and started forwarding/resolving encoded refs through BrainRoot registration, BrainIoInfo/BrainRoutingInfo, output-sink updates, RegionShard output-sink consumption, IoGateway router/coordinator resolution, WorkerNode shard/output publication, and BrainSignalRouter routing-table/register-IoGateway resolution. RoutingTableSnapshot now carries an optional actor-reference alongside the concrete PID so receivers can resolve shard routes from their own network vantage point instead of reusing HiveMind’s chosen transport path. Added focused tests for BrainRoot routable registration publication and encoded output-sink forwarding; full suite passed (1022 tests). Remaining work is now narrower: some runtime/control-plane surfaces still preserve only concrete PIDs or addresses outside this migrated path, and mixed-topology transparency is substantially closer but not fully complete until those remaining fields are lifted onto the same logical-ref/resolution model.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 9: extended mixed-topology routing into worker placement/inventory paths. Added compatibility worker_actor_reference fields to PlacementWorkerInventoryEntry and PlacementPeerTarget, taught HiveMind to learn node endpoint sets from settings and derive routable worker root references from them, switched placement dispatch and worker-capability refresh to resolve worker targets from those references, and switched inter-worker peer-latency probes to carry/resolve worker actor refs instead of trusting only worker_address. HiveMind also now subscribes to SettingsMonitor with a routable subscriber reference when local endpoint candidates are available. Added focused tests for routable HiveMind settings subscription, worker inventory actor-reference publication, worker peer-latency actor-reference preference, and proto compatibility. Full suite passed (1025 tests). Remaining work: some runtime/discovery callback paths still publish or subscribe with plain concrete PIDs/addresses, and a few mixed-topology control-plane surfaces remain outside the logical-ref model.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 10: carried mixed-topology routing into live discovery subscriptions. ServiceEndpointDiscoveryClient now accepts local endpoint candidates and publishes routable subscriber references for SettingSubscribe/SettingUnsubscribe when available, while preserving legacy plain-PID behavior for callers that do not pass candidates. Wired IO, WorkerNode, Reproduction, and Speciation runtime entry points to pass their local endpoint candidates into discovery client creation so live SettingsMonitor endpoint updates can route back across mixed TailScale/LAN/public topologies the same way other control-plane callbacks now do. Added focused discovery-client coverage for encoded subscriber refs and reran discovery/runtime integration coverage; full suite passed (1026 tests). Remaining work is now narrower: some callback/discovery surfaces still use plain concrete addresses or PID labels outside these migrated paths, but the major settings/discovery/control-plane loops now have end-to-end routable-reference support.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress this turn, slice 11: closed the IO output replay gap for mixed-topology callback delivery. IoGateway now stores both the resolved sender PID and the original subscriber actor reference for output subscriptions, keyed by logical subscriber identity, so replay to a replacement output coordinator preserves the original encoded routable reference instead of flattening it to one concrete PID label. Added a focused integration regression covering output-coordinator replacement with an encoded subscriber ref and reran the full suite; full suite passed (1027 tests). Remaining work is now mostly the smaller leftover callback/control-plane surfaces that still only remember concrete addresses or PID labels outside the main migrated loops.
