title: Optional energy/cost-modulated plasticity signal
description:
Add an optional energy/cost modulation path for plasticity updates. This is intentionally separate from core plasticity behavior so it can be evaluated and tuned in isolation.

Intent:
- Use energy/cost context as a bounded modulation signal (update gating and/or magnitude scaling).
- Encourage efficiency and reduce runaway high-cost activity patterns.
- Preserve backward compatibility: when disabled, behavior must match baseline plasticity semantics.

This is a policy/control issue, not a replacement for core plasticity logic.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Config/proto/runtime surfaces are added for optional energy/cost modulation with explicit bounds.
2) Deterministic runtime behavior applies modulation consistently when enabled.
3) Disabled mode remains behaviorally equivalent to baseline plasticity.
4) Tests show enabled modulation reduces pathological high-cost dynamics while preserving useful input responsiveness.
5) Operator/docs guidance explains tradeoffs and safe defaults.
close_reason: Implemented and validated

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Scope boundary update from decay/homeostasis discussion (2026-03-01):

Keep NBNv2-a1t focused on plasticity-only energy/cost modulation.
Homeostasis/decay energy coupling should be tracked separately (new runtime issue under NBNv2-zwv) so plasticity and buffer-state homeostasis can be tuned/validated independently.

Shared policy expectation across both surfaces:
- modulation is optional and bounded
- disabled mode preserves baseline behavior
- deterministic behavior under fixed seeds/ticks
- operator-facing defaults/safety guidance documented

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
﻿Follow-up note after completing NBNv2-zwv.8:

Plasticity baseline now exposes separate controls for:
- rate (probabilistic gating)
- delta (magnitude step)
- deterministic auto-rebase thresholds (count/fraction of changed codes)

a1t integration guidance:
- Apply optional energy/cost modulation as bounded scaling/offset on effective rate and/or delta before plasticity update execution.
- Keep rebase-threshold mechanics unchanged by default; modulation should affect mutation behavior, not overlay bookkeeping semantics.
- Disabled mode must remain behaviorally identical to zwv.8 baseline (exact deterministic parity under same seed/ticks).
- Add tests that compare enabled vs disabled modulation on identical seeds and verify baseline parity when disabled.

Added runtime/integration/proto tests (including disabled parity and high-cost suppression behavior) and docs updates. Full Release tests passed (545/545).

description: Current fix clears coordinator/shard state and rejects reset while router tick work is already pending, but reset is still initiated out-of-band from IO/router rather than at the authoritative HiveMind per-brain deliver-to-compute barrier. Follow up by moving reset coordination to the barrier so shard/coordinator clearing is atomic with respect to tick progression.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reset execution is coordinated at the per-brain HiveMind tick barrier rather than as an out-of-band IO mutation. 2. Reset cannot interleave with pending outbox delivery or input drain replay for the same brain. 3. End-to-end tests cover successful reset and busy/retry behavior across distributed coordinators/shards.
close_reason: Queued whole-brain runtime reset in HiveMind, applied it at the per-brain deliver-to-compute barrier via IO, added busy/retry handling, and covered barrier/distributed reset paths with regression tests.

title: Follow up invalid application/x-nbn artifact-store indexing overflow on malformed bytes
description: While implementing NBNv2-zwv.31 coverage, LocalArtifactStore.StoreAsync auto-populated region indexes for seekable application/x-nbn payloads and ArtifactRegionIndexBuilder overflowed on malformed bytes instead of failing with a clearer validation error. Decide whether invalid .nbn stores should be rejected earlier/explicitly and add coverage.
close_reason: Best-effort malformed/out-of-range .nbn auto-index handling implemented with regression coverage and docs updates.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed. ArtifactRegionIndexBuilder auto-indexing is now best-effort for seekable application/x-nbn writes: malformed or out-of-range header metadata no longer fails StoreAsync and no persisted region index is written in those cases. Added malformed-header and out-of-range offset regression coverage, updated artifact-store docs/spec text, and validated with focused artifact tests plus a clean 847/847 full-suite run.

title: Designer: Fix region canvas not updating for new/other neurons

title: Prevent Workbench local launches from using stale Release binaries
description: Workbench currently starts local runtime services from existing Release binaries or dotnet run --no-build. During NBNv2-bqd verification this caused a live EvolutionSim run to execute stale code even though source had changed, which hid the runtime fix in the new log run.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Local Workbench launches detect stale project outputs before start and rebuild or fall back to a build path. 2. EvolutionSim and orchestrated services no longer silently run outdated Release binaries after source edits. 3. Add a focused regression or launch-path test where practical, or document the invariant if automated coverage is not feasible.
close_reason: Completed: Workbench local launches now preflight target projects with a Release build before start, so Orchestrator services and EvolutionSim no longer silently run stale Release outputs after source edits; focused launch regressions and docs updates included.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed after reviewing epic context (NBNv2-zwv root plus NBNv2-zwv.21-.27) and keeping the runtime-first alignment policy in scope. Implemented a shared Workbench local-launch preflight that runs a Release build before starting local services/EvolutionSim, then launches the fresh executable or a post-build dotnet run --no-build fallback if no executable output exists. Wired Orchestrator and Speciation-pane simulator launches through that preflight, added focused regressions for the preparer and launch-failure reporting, and updated Workbench design docs to record the invariant that local launch must not silently reuse stale Release outputs after source edits. Validation: dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp --filter FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Workbench.LocalProjectLaunchPreparerTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Workbench.OrchestratorPanelViewModelTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Workbench.SpeciationPanelViewModelTests (78/78); dotnet build -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp; dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (755 passed).

title: Investigate intermittent speciation bootstrap-fallback provenance test flake
description: Observed once on 2026-03-10 during the first full-suite validation for NBNv2-zwv.2: SpeciationManagerActorTests.ProtoAssign_Commit_NewbornDerivedSpecies_BootstrapFallbackPersistsTimeoutCompatibilityProvenance failed, then passed on immediate isolated rerun and on the subsequent full-suite rerun. This should be treated as a real runtime or async-ordering stability issue until disproven, not a test-only normalization problem.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Reproduce or otherwise isolate the intermittent failure path for ProtoAssign_Commit_NewbornDerivedSpecies_BootstrapFallbackPersistsTimeoutCompatibilityProvenance. 2) Identify whether the instability is in runtime behavior, persistence timing, or test harness ordering. 3) Land a fix that stabilizes the scenario under repeated suite execution, with regression coverage if the root cause is in production code.
close_reason: Isolated and fixed the intermittent bootstrap-fallback timeout-provenance flake by correcting runtime timeout elapsed-ms serialization in SpeciationManagerActor, strengthening regression coverage, and validating with targeted, slice, and full-suite runs.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Isolated the intermittent bootstrap-fallback timeout-provenance failure to runtime elapsed-ms reporting at the timeout boundary, not to speciation decision logic or persistence ordering. In SpeciationManagerActor, bootstrap assigned-species admission metadata was serializing elapsed time from a truncating stopwatch sample, so a real compatibility timeout could be recorded just under the configured timeout budget. Fixed production code to round elapsed milliseconds upward and floor timeout failures at the configured compatibility request timeout before persisting ssigned_species_compatibility_elapsed_ms. Hardened regression coverage by asserting the timeout-path request count is exactly one and by documenting the timeout-floor provenance behavior in the local Speciation design doc. Validation: nearby bootstrap/speciation tests passed (4/4), full speciation slice passed (68/68), full Nbn.Tests suite passed (779/779), and the post-fix stress loop completed 23 interrupted iterations of the exact flaky test with 0 failures before operator stop. Additional isolation evidence: a pre-fix 40-iteration isolated loop also stayed green, which is consistent with a narrow timing-boundary serialization defect rather than broken branch logic.

During NBNv2-8jc verification on 2026-03-22, the full solution suite failed once with ack == null at tests/Nbn.Tests/Workbench/DesignerPanelArtifactStoreTests.cs:343, while an immediate isolated rerun and a full-suite rerun both passed. comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Reproduced the restore flake locally in a short loop after the original isolated rerun passed. Root cause was test-harness sensitivity around the Designer restore path's reachable artifact publisher port: the test was reusing the default adjacent artifact port and could intermittently fail before RequestPlacement completed. Stabilized by running DesignerPanelArtifactStoreTests under the existing ArtifactEnvSerial collection and reserving a unique reachable artifact port for the restore-forwarding test. title: Support multi-address runtime advertisement for mixed network topologies
close_reason: Superseded and implemented by the mixed-topology rollout tracked through NBNv2-8ga and follow-up cleanup in NBNv2-4id; no separate open work remains under this umbrella.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Current runtime discovery and Proto.Actor remoting publish a single host:port/actor address per service/node. Mixed deployments where some peers can only reach TailScale addresses and others can only reach LAN/public addresses need multi-address publication plus endpoint selection/routing changes rather than a single advertise-host override.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Scoped mixed-topology design: move from single host:port/actor publication to repeated endpoint candidates per node/service/callback publisher, keep stable logical node/service ids, and add caller-side endpoint resolution with probe/cache-based candidate selection. Migration should start in SettingsMonitor discovery + Workbench/observability/placement-reconcile consumers, then move runtime control-plane payloads that currently embed raw PID strings or single addresses (HiveMind runtime surfaces, WorkerNode hosting/reconcile, IO gateway registration/routing, callback subscriber registrations). Goal UX: operator enters only reachable Settings coordinates; peers auto-select the best published route per caller. Constraint: every peer still needs at least one mutually reachable candidate; transparency cannot bypass absent network reachability.

title: Fix missing viz activity events (VizTick-only from region 0)
description:
Current reproducible bug (user retest on 2026-02-27):
- Brain spawn succeeds.
- Hosted workers are visible.
- Brain graph renders with expected nodes/edges.
- Visualization Stream emits only VIZ_TICK events, only from region 0.
- No neuron/axon activity events are observed in stream or visual activity overlay.

This issue is now scoped only to missing activity emission/propagation (not spawn visibility or graph structure).
acceptance_criteria:
1. In a fresh local Workbench run (new instance), spawn an activity-seeded random brain and subscribe to visualization.
2. Within 10 seconds, stream contains non-tick events (VIZ_NEURON_BUFFER and/or VIZ_NEURON_FIRED and/or VIZ_AXON_SENT).
3. Non-tick events are observed from at least one non-zero region (not region 0 only).
4. Brain visualization shows corresponding runtime activity (not tick-only heartbeat).
5. Evidence captured in latest Workbench logs for one passing run.
notes:
Trimmed scope: only the remaining VizTick-only activity-path failure.
Historical fixes (descriptor registration, subscriber cleanup, output width handling, etc.) are background context and are not active acceptance criteria for this issue.
Reproduction abort behavior is tracked separately and is out of scope here.
close_reason: Resolved: visualization activity events now stream and render; remaining label overlap tracked separately if needed.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Fixes: add descriptors and extract shared config to src/Nbn.Runtime.WorkerNode/WorkerNodeRemote.cs; Program now uses WorkerNodeRemote.BuildConfig(...). Added integration transport regression tests: RemoteDescriptorTransportTests.WorkerNodeRemote_Transports_VisualizationEvent_Messages and WorkerNodeRemote_Transports_DebugOutbound_Messages. Also fixed related export path timeout by adding nbn_io to HiveMindRemote (affects full-brain definition hydration). Waiting for user UI retest before closure.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Key changes: (1) control.SetBrainVisualization now carries subscriber_actor; WorkbenchClient now always sends stable receiver PID identity, (2) HiveMind tracks per-brain subscriber preferences and computes effective scope (full-brain if any full-brain subscriber or conflicting focused regions; focused region only when all subscribers agree), (3) un/subscribing one client no longer disables visualization for other active subscribers, (4) cleanup path now includes death-watch handling + periodic sweep using SettingsMonitor NodeList (known node addresses that are no longer alive) + local process-registry liveness fallback for local subscribers, (5) docs updated to canonize Observability as shared core service with single DebugHub/VisualizationHub per deployment. HiveMind now requests SettingsMonitor NodeList alongside worker inventory refresh and tracks known/active node addresses; sweep removes subscriber leases tied to known cluster node addresses that are no longer alive. Added tests: SetBrainVisualization_TerminatedSubscriberMessage_RemovesOnlyTargetedSubscriber and SetBrainVisualization_StoppedLocalSubscriber_IsEventuallyCleanedUp (no skips).

User reports that random-brain activity without manual input vectors was previously present/visualized in most or all random brains before the recent closed Beads epic cluster. This means the baseline driver is useful as a fallback but should not be treated as inherently required for random-brain activity.

title: Investigate founder-species collapse for initial/manual EvolutionSim parents
description:
Repro: in Workbench EvolutionSim, add two instances of one random brain and two instances of a second random brain as parents (four total seed entries, two unique parents) and run with speciation commit enabled. Expected behavior is that the two clearly distinct founders separate into at least two species early, so their immediate children inherit two founder lineages. Observed behavior is that Speciation often keeps the initial parents and their immediate children in one species instead of splitting by founder as expected.

Current code evidence:
- tools/Nbn.Tools.EvolutionSim/EvolutionSimulationSession.cs seeds initial parents one at a time in SeedInitialParentsAsync before iteration commits.
- tools/Nbn.Tools.EvolutionSim/EvolutionRuntimeClient.cs sends each seed/manual commit through the normal SpeciationAssign commit path with candidate=parent and parent refs = parent + selected partner.
- src/Nbn.Runtime.Speciation/SpeciationManagerActor.cs ResolveAssignment falls back to the default species with decision reason lineage_unavailable_default when requested species is empty and current-epoch parent membership evidence is unavailable.
- tools/Nbn.Tools.EvolutionSim/Design.md says seed parents are committed before iterations specifically so later assignments have parent membership evidence, but there is no regression covering two distinct initial/manual parents committed without explicit species IDs.

Hypothesis:
- We may need an initial founder-speciation/clustering pass over unique initial parents before ordinary seeding, OR
- a dedicated assignment path for manually added/seed parents whose commits do not yet have existing parent membership info in the current epoch, instead of routing them straight into the lineage_unavailable_default fallback.

Acceptance clues:
1. Two distinct initial/manual parents without explicit species IDs no longer collapse into one default species when they are used as EvolutionSim seed parents.
2. Immediate children preserve founder separation until later lineage evidence justifies reuse/merge.
3. Add a regression in SpeciationManagerActorTests and/or EvolutionSimulationSessionTests for this exact two-founder seed-parent scenario.
notes: Reopened after live-log/DB verification: in brain_id launch mode with spawn_children=false, successful committed artifact children were never re-entering the simulator parent pool, and mixed brain/artifact pairs were rejected downstream by EvolutionRuntimeClient. Result: parent_pool_size stayed fixed at the initial founders, children_added_to_pool stayed 0, epoch 1 stayed one species and epoch 2 stayed only the two root species.
close_reason: Completed: brain-mode simulator runs with spawn_children=false now re-add committed artifact children to the parent pool, mixed brain/artifact pairs reproduce via artifact fallback, and multi-root/single-root sessions no longer stall with children_added_to_pool stuck at zero.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented runtime-first fix for founder-species collapse in EvolutionSim seed parents. Seed commits now use assessment-derived founder pair similarity instead of synthetic perfect similarity once a prior founder exists, which lets a distinct second/manual founder split from the first founder instead of inheriting the default species by default. Added regressions in SpeciationManagerActorTests and EvolutionSimulationSessionTests, and updated EvolutionSim design docs. Validation: dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp --filter FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Tools.EvolutionSimulationSessionTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Speciation.SpeciationManagerActorTests; dotnet build -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp; dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (737 passed).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up fix after live-log verification: simulator/speciation now key SHA-backed artifact parents and candidates by content hash instead of store_uri, so equivalent exports seed/resolve as the same founder lineage. Also added optional use of request species_display_name for lineage-less default assignments only; lineage-backed assignments ignore it unless species_id is explicit. Validation: targeted speciation/evolutionsim tests passed (70), full build passed, full test suite passed (740).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Second live-log follow-up fix: self-parent founder splits now create independent root species instead of lineage_diverged_new_species branches from the first founder, so Workbench cladograms no longer show one initial founder as descended from the other. EvolutionSim also normalizes all species/lineage families first discovered during the initial seed batch to the same selection age, removing seed-order bias from early stability/divergence parent selection. Added brain/artifact founder regressions plus a simulator selection-bias regression. Validation: targeted speciation/evolutionsim tests passed (72), full build passed, full test suite passed (742), and Release outputs were rebuilt for Workbench.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up for multi-root naming and parent-selection behavior

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Runtime changes: numbered root species display names when multiple initial roots exist; descendant lineage labels now inherit a root-specific ordinal prefix so compact chart labels remain bracket-only but root-unique; multi-lineage-family parent selection now preserves lineage-family balancing while restoring within-family species competition using family-relative species age. comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed follow-up fix for the brain-mode pool-growth stall. Evidence from the latest Workbench run: epoch 1 accumulated many species_membership decisions but only unclassified in the DB, epoch 2 contained only the two root species, and EvolutionSim status logs showed eproduction_calls and speciation_commit_successes increasing while children_added_to_pool remained 0. Runtime changes: EvolutionSimulationSession now admits committed artifact children back into brain-mode parent pools when spawn_children=false, and EvolutionRuntimeClient now resolves mixed brain/artifact parent pairs through artifact requests by reading BrainInfo.BaseDefinition and falling back to ExportBrainDefinition when needed. This preserves distinct live seed founders while restoring pool turnover and downstream speciation. Validation: targeted EvolutionSimulationSession/EvolutionRuntimeClientMetadata tests passed (35), full build passed with 0 warnings/0 errors, full test suite passed (750), and normal Release outputs were rebuilt.

title: Add compatibility-only match assessment and fixed reproduction run_count
description:
Add a dedicated compatibility assessment path so external callers can request match/similarity metrics without generating or spawning a child.

Also add a fixed run-count option on reproduction requests so callers can ask runtime to execute reproduction N times in one request (default 1), instead of issuing N separate calls.

Intent:
- External systems can do compatibility planning from match % first.
- External systems can then choose how many children to create using their own policy logic.

Out of scope for this issue:
- Session persistence/cached match tickets between calls.
- Caller-supplied formula execution inside runtime.
- Seed-policy redesign for compatibility scoring.
acceptance_criteria:
- Introduce external compatibility-assessment requests that return similarity/compatibility output without child synthesis, artifact creation, or spawn attempts.
- Support both existing parent addressing modes (by brain IDs and by artifact refs) for assessment.
- Assessment response includes compatibility flag, abort reason (if any), and component scores used for match decisions.
- Add a fixed reproduction run count field (for example `run_count`) to external reproduction requests with default behavior equivalent to 1 run.
- For run_count > 1, runtime executes that many independent reproduction runs and returns deterministic ordering of per-run outcomes.
- Existing callers that omit run_count continue to behave as today.
- Validation enforces sane run_count bounds and surfaces clear abort/failure reasons on invalid requests.
- Add/adjust tests for:
  - assessment-only success/abort paths,
  - no-child/no-spawn guarantee in assessment mode,
  - run_count default behavior,
  - run_count > 1 response shape and per-run outcome reporting,
  - invalid run_count handling.
- Update canonical docs and protocol schema docs for new assessment requests/responses and run_count behavior.
close_reason: Completed runtime/proto/docs/tests for compatibility-only assessment and run_count multi-run reproduction

title: Investigate transient timeout in HandleBrainTerminated_NonEnergyReason_Uses_LocalEnergyState_ForBroadcast
description:
Observed once on 2026-02-26 during local targeted test run with --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp while Workbench/runtime processes were active.

Failure:
- Test: Nbn.Tests.Integration.IoGatewayArtifactReferenceTests.HandleBrainTerminated_NonEnergyReason_Uses_LocalEnergyState_ForBroadcast
- Exception: System.Threading.Tasks.TaskCanceledException
- File/line: tests/Nbn.Tests/Integration/IoGatewayArtifactReferenceTests.cs:990

Immediate rerun of this exact test passed, and rerun of the full targeted slice passed (39/39), so this is currently treated as transient/flaky until root cause is identified.

Follow-up scope:
1. Capture deterministic repro conditions (runtime load, active process count, timing).
2. Add additional diagnostics around the timeout path in this test/harness.
3. Stabilize assertions or coordination timing if race is confirmed.
close_reason: Not reproducible after repeated validation; treated as addressed by recent flaky-test fixes (commit 8eae7ae).

title: Persist brain artifact provenance for offline speciation admission checks
description: Current speciation now gates external continuity into an existing derived species through reproduction compatibility assessments, persisting machine-usable artifact refs for artifact candidates and using live brain IDs when available. Remaining gap: brain-id memberships do not persist their resolved base/snapshot artifact provenance, so later admission checks can fail once those brains are no longer resolvable through IO/reproduction discovery. Capture or persist enough artifact provenance for brain-id memberships to allow assessment-only compatibility checks even after the original live brain is gone, without weakening determinism or reintroducing heuristic-only continuity.
close_reason: Completed: persisted brain-id artifact provenance for offline/bootstrap compatibility admission with runtime, test, and docs validation.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Fresh live March 7, 2026 run shows a separate bootstrap gap beyond artifact provenance. In C:\\Users\\Haile\\AppData\\Local\\Nbn.Workbench\\speciation.db there are 126 memberships across 7 species and zero lineage.assigned_species_similarity_source=compatibility_assessment entries in the first 10 members of any derived species (in fact zero across the entire epoch). Example derived species unclassified-branch-e5d37fafd514 seeds its first non-founder floor sample at 0.8602426 with policy threshold 0.86, so split proximity stays near zero despite improved anti-fragmentation behavior. This points to newborn-species floor seeding still relying on heuristic in-species parent scores rather than actual compatibility samples.

Speciation now discovers the IO Gateway, resolves live brain base/snapshot artifact refs for committed brain_id memberships, persists machine-usable candidate_brain_base_artifact_ref and candidate_brain_snapshot_artifact_ref in decision metadata, and prefers artifact-mode compatibility assessment when stored brain-id exemplars/candidates are no longer live-discoverable. Added regression coverage for founder-goes-offline bootstrap admission and updated speciation design/canonical protocol docs. title: Require actual similarity admission checks for newborn species
description: Current speciation runtime still assigns newborn-species continuity from lineage/parent-based similarity evidence only. It does not compute fresh candidate-vs-existing-member similarity inside speciation, which means a newborn species can be grown using heuristic fit rather than actual intra-species similarity. Implement runtime-first admission checks so recent realign/reuse or other continuity paths into a newborn/derived species require real similarity assessment against that derived species rather than only source-lineage estimates. Keep source-species split pressure and assigned-species cohesion distinct. Preserve deterministic/replay-safe behavior, add regression coverage, and update docs/operator interpretation.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented runtime-first derived-species admission checks. Speciation now discovers the reproduction manager, issues assessment-only compatibility requests before recent realign/reuse or hindsight reassignment can admit a candidate into an existing derived species, rejects those continuity paths when the real score is unavailable/not above the target threshold/not better than source, and persists machine-usable candidate artifact refs in decision metadata for artifact candidates. Added regression coverage for positive and negative recent realign/hindsight/reuse paths plus artifact metadata persistence. Validation: targeted SpeciationManagerActorTests passed (33/33), full dotnet test passed (702/702), and docs render/check passed.

comments.text: Completed. Added a cross-process/shared-root artifact-store regression harness using a test-only worker process plus parent-side process launcher. New coverage verifies: (1) overlapping shared-chunk writes across two OS processes reuse chunk metadata safely, (2) duplicate-byte .nbn stores across processes persist merged region-index metadata, and (3) a real SQLite write lock held by another process causes StoreAsync to wait and succeed instead of surfacing immediate SQLITE_BUSY. Added busy_timeout configuration on artifact DB connections and documented the remaining limit that node-local cache roots are still per-process/per-node rather than a shared coherence layer. title: Align EvolutionSim sim_commit with committed source-species similarity

title: Broaden random generation/reproduction usefulness: autonomous baseline + input-range responsiveness
description:
Random generation should produce brains that are varied and evolution-useful, not unconstrained randomness with only a few hard constraints. Expand hg4 to improve random generation and applicable reproduction defaults/ranges/options with guided stochastic shaping: broad structural/function decisions first, then narrower sampling within sane ranges and constraints.

Behavior intent:
- No-input runs: brains usually show low-but-nonzero activity (not dead, not saturated).
- Input-aware runs: given typical external input values in [-1, 1] (continuous, periodic, occasional, or irregular), at least some input and output neurons should usually activate after a not-too-large number of input updates, with output activation following after a reasonable delay.
- Output should vary with recent input history rather than behaving as near-constant noise.
- Diversity remains real under default settings and under reasonable user-provided region/neuron/axon limits.

This issue is broad quality shaping work for random generation/reproduction defaults and option surfaces (usefulness, default behavior, ranges, value choices, and generation steps), while preserving deterministic reproducibility per seed and all NBN invariants.

Non-goals:
- Do not turn random generation into fully unconstrained randomness.
- Do not introduce a full training algorithm in this issue.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Deterministic seed-suite smoke tests show observable no-input activity within bounded ranges (minimum activity floor and anti-saturation ceiling).
2) Input-path smoke tests using values in [-1, 1] show increased activity and non-trivial output variation within bounded ticks versus no-input baselines.
3) Under continuous, periodic, and irregular input cadences, activation remains viable without requiring per-tick input.
4) Cross-seed diversity checks show non-trivial variation in topology/function/connectivity patterns under defaults.
5) Any reproduction default/range adjustments included by this work are documented and validated against invariants.
6) Tests are stable and non-flaky across repeated runs.
close_reason: Completed with runtime implementation, deterministic distribution + dynamic responsiveness coverage, and design-doc update.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Design update from decay/homeostasis discussion (2026-03-01):

1) Random generation defaults (internal/non-IO regions) should move from broad reset-function sampling to a curated default subset focused on stable fading-memory behavior.
2) Proposed default weighted subset for INTERNAL regions: RESET_HOLD 35%, RESET_HALF 30%, RESET_TENTH 20%, RESET_HUNDREDTH 10%, RESET_ZERO 5%.
3) Keep existing IO guardrails: input/output regions remain RESET_ZERO in non-fixed modes; fixed mode can still override explicitly.
4) Reproduction remains free to use the full reset-function space (no curated restriction there).
5) Implementation should include deterministic seed-based tests validating distribution, non-dead baseline dynamics, and no regression in IO-region guardrails.

Rationale: this improves default random usefulness while avoiding uncontrolled function-space sampling for initial brains.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Linkage: runtime homeostasis behavior is tracked in NBNv2-zwv.19. This hg4 item should consume that runtime capability by adjusting random internal reset defaults/weights (curated subset) while keeping reproduction function-space breadth.

comments.text: Implemented hg4 runtime + coverage pass: (1) random internal reset pool in non-fixed modes is now curated to RESET_HOLD/RESET_HALF/RESET_TENTH/RESET_HUNDREDTH/RESET_ZERO with weighted defaults 35/30/20/10/5, (2) output-region resets in non-fixed modes are now pinned to RESET_ZERO while fixed mode still honors explicit reset IDs, (3) random generation now guarantees an input->output influence path when inter-region links are enabled and axons are allowed. Added deterministic seed-suite tests for curated reset distribution + IO guardrails and runtime smoke tests for no-input bounded activity and cadence-based input responsiveness/variation using RegionShardCpuBackend simulation. Verified with: dotnet test --filter FullyQualifiedName~DesignerPanelRandomBrainTests (13 passed), dotnet test --filter FullyQualifiedName~DesignerPanel (19 passed), and dotnet build -c Release --disable-build-servers.

Runtime now clamps buffer/potential/reset/contribution flow to bounded signal range and normalizes threshold gating safety. Random generation now favors dense intra/sparse inter topology, uses a bounded internal activation subset, keeps output accumulation in SUM for better signed responsiveness, seeds stronger guided input->output influence paths, and adds high-likelihood output->input-endpoint recurrence bridges (outside region 0) with one-to-one target preference. Added expanded deterministic tests for bounded dynamics, lagged input-output responsiveness, and recurrence/connectivity structure. Validation: dotnet test --filter FullyQualifiedName~DesignerPanelRandomBrainTests (14 passed), --filter FullyQualifiedName~DesignerPanel (20 passed), --filter FullyQualifiedName~RegionShardCpuBackend (38 passed), plus dotnet build -c Release.

title: Make IO-region neuron-count protection optional with explicit manual overrides
description:
Current behavior/docs treat neuron add/remove in regions 0 and 31 as an absolute reproduction prohibition.

Implement a configurable policy where this protection is default-enabled but optional, and expose request-level manual edits so external callers can explicitly add/remove neurons in input/output regions when the protection is disabled.

Scope includes runtime validation, protocol/API surface, and deterministic behavior alignment with existing IO axon invariants.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a reproduction policy option (for example `protect_io_region_neuron_counts`) with default enabled behavior matching current restriction.
- Extend external reproduction request contracts to accept explicit manual neuron add/remove operations for regions 0 and/or 31.
- When protection is enabled, reject any region 0/31 neuron add/remove attempts (including manual lists).
- When protection is disabled, allow region 0/31 neuron add/remove only from explicit caller-provided manual operations; stochastic mutation probabilities must not introduce them.
- Keep existing IO-region axon invariants and duplicate-target rules enforced for all outcomes.
- Add/adjust tests for default-protected behavior, override-enabled behavior, invalid request paths, and deterministic result reporting.
- Update canonical docs and protocol docs for the new option and request fields.
close_reason: Completed: implemented io-region protection policy + manual override contract/runtime behavior, tests, and docs.

title: Split NBNv2_HumanGuide_TEMP.md into canonical split docs with zero detail loss
description:
﻿## Summary
Split `docs/temp/NBNv2_HumanGuide_TEMP.md` into canonical, domain-scoped documentation files while preserving complete technical fidelity.

## Why this is required
- The repository uses `docs/INDEX.md` as composition template and `docs/NBNv2.md` as rendered full document.
- Canonical split docs now live in repo-root `spec/sections/*` and project-local docs near code (`src/*/Docs/Design.md`, `tools/*/(Docs|docs)/Design.md`, `tests/*/Docs/Design.md`).
- The split must not lose any requirements, constraints, formulas, protocols, file-format details, defaults, or roadmap/troubleshooting content.

## Scope
- Source input: `docs/temp/NBNv2_HumanGuide_TEMP.md`.
- Target canonical docs:
  - `spec/sections/overview/*.md`
  - `spec/sections/runtime/*.md`
  - `spec/sections/formats/*.md`
  - `spec/sections/protocols/*.md`
  - `spec/sections/reference/*.md`
  - project docs in `src/*/Docs/Design.md`, `tools/*/(Docs|docs)/Design.md`, `tests/*/Docs/Design.md`
- Keep `docs/INDEX.md` readable with transition prose and include markers.
- Keep `docs/manifest/NBNv2-DocumentMap.md` synchronized with final split ownership and assembly order.

## Required implementation details
1. Perform full-fidelity migration of all content from the temporary guide into split docs.
2. Preserve every normative requirement and invariant, including:
   - region and IO connectivity invariants
   - tick-phase barrier and timeout/lateness behavior
   - cost/energy model rules
   - distance formulas/constants
   - sharding/placement constraints
   - backend behavior and parity assumptions
   - plasticity behavior
   - lifecycle/snapshot/recovery behavior
   - artifact store and dedup behavior
   - complete `.nbn` and `.nbs` format details (layout/bitfields/formulas)
   - database schema details
   - complete `.proto` contracts
   - roadmap/demo/troubleshooting notes
   - appendices (defaults/function catalog/region map)
3. Rewriting for flow is allowed, but semantics must remain equivalent or become more explicit.
4. Where code diverges from temporary-guide text, update docs to current behavior by adding clarity rather than removing capability detail.
5. Keep project docs concise and decision-focused; avoid adding transient operational noise.
6. Keep `docs/temp/NBNv2_HumanGuide_TEMP.md` intact until split verification is complete.

## Verification checklist
- Coverage audit against temporary guide shows no dropped topic/section.
- All include paths referenced by `docs/INDEX.md` resolve.
- `docs/manifest/NBNv2-DocumentMap.md` reflects final assembly order.
- Rendered `docs/NBNv2.md` contains full intended specification content with no missing sections.
- Terminology and contract names match current code/proto names.

## Out of scope
- Render automation/GitHub Actions pipeline (tracked separately).
- README rewrite (tracked separately).
close_reason: Closed

Migrate any still-relevant technical decisions/details into canonical split docs. Only delete/archive supplementary docs after coverage verification confirms the retained information is present (or intentionally superseded) in canonical docs.

title: Investigate remote-only spawn retry exhaustion on non-GPU worker after recent placement/backend changes
description: User-reported regression: when core services run on one host and the only worker is a remote non-GPU machine, Workbench spawn sometimes fails with timeout/retry-exhausted behavior even though the worker appears online and ready. Reverse topology with this GPU-capable machine as the remote worker succeeds. Investigate recent placement/backend changes (including ILGPU/backend preference and worker capability selection), sender/endpoint attribution, and remote worker metadata/assignment failure modes. Fix the real runtime issue and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Fixed Workbench reachable-artifact publisher to use a stable firewallable port for remote spawn flows; regression tests added.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Root cause tracked to Workbench-hosted reachable spawn artifacts using an ad hoc HTTP server on an ephemeral port. In mixed-host setups the remote worker could stay online/ready yet fail shard assignment with retryable artifact-metadata misses until HiveMind exhausted the retry budget, because the cross-machine firewall allowances covered the core service ports but not the random artifact-store port. Fixed by making Workbench artifact publication reuse a stable reachable HTTP port adjacent to the Workbench client port, sharing one hosted store across backing roots, and keeping local artifact copies. Added regressions for stable-port reuse and connection-derived artifact-port resolution. Verified with targeted Workbench tests (81/81) and docs freshness check.

title: Do not skip ticks when routing snapshot is empty

acceptance_criteria: Brains with registered shards participate in tick barrier even if routing snapshot is empty; compute/deliver dispatch no longer returns early solely due to empty snapshot.

title: Follow up on intermittent rapid-spawn placement stalls after NBNv2-4mp
description:
Even after the queued-ack / placement-ready split, worker-lane queueing, explicit placement reply routing, and faster designer-managed placement timeout handling, some rapid same-worker spawns still intermittently stall before completing placement.

Observed latest behavior from local Workbench runs:
- early spawns in a burst can place quickly
- a later spawn sometimes logs only Placement requested and then never reaches shard/output registration before the designer-managed 5s placement wait expires
- the user can retry quickly now, but the underlying runtime stall still needs root-cause work

Investigate remaining runtime causes in the WorkerNode/HiveMind placement path, especially later same-worker bursts where a brain never becomes placement-visible despite earlier brains in the same run succeeding.

This yields all-zero thresholds/params and identical functions across all neurons, producing mostly zero output unless manual inputs are sent. Consider switching defaults to Random/Weighted for functions and non-zero ranges for thresholds/params, or clarify in UI.

title: Bootstrap derived-species floors from actual compatibility samples
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Fresh live March 7, 2026 evidence from C:\\Users\\Haile\\AppData\\Local\\Nbn.Workbench\\speciation.db: 126 memberships across 7 species; zero lineage.assigned_species_similarity_source=compatibility_assessment entries in the first 10 members of any derived species, and zero across the full epoch. For unclassified-branch-e5d37fafd514, founder rows stay synthetic at 1.0 then the first non-founder local sample is 0.8602426 against a 0.86 policy threshold, so split proximity remains ~0 to 0.05 while the species floor is seeded from heuristic parent-pair scores. Need runtime-first fix: for the first non-founder admissions into a newborn derived species, force actual compatibility/manual similarity checks against founder/earliest species members, feed those real scores into lineage_assignment_similarity_score/intra_species_similarity_sample/species-floor state, and block source-similarity carryover until enough in-species actual samples exist.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented runtime bootstrap gating for newborn derived species. Speciation now treats a derived species as unbootstrapped until it records a non-founder compatibility-backed in-species sample; during that window every admission into the derived species is forced through an assessment-only reproduction compatibility check against the earliest members, and failures fall back to the pre-split source species instead of seeding the floor from heuristics. Runtime also tracks compatibility-backed floor samples through replay/startup, suppresses synthetic founder floor seeding unless the admission is compatibility-backed, and blocks source-similarity carryover during bootstrap. Validation: speciation slice 35/35, full suite 704/704, docs render/check passed.

title: Repository-wide maintainability refactor without behavioral drift
description:
Goal and scope:
Complete a repository-wide maintainability refactor across handwritten code in src/, tools/, tests/, and affected repo-root config/docs so the codebase is more consistent, easier to review, and easier to extend without changing externally visible behavior.

Scope includes:
- consistent style and organization across runtime, shared, tooling, and tests
- Proto.Actor usage cleanup and standardization
- dead code / stale comment / placeholder removal
- concise XML documentation for public and protected APIs
- test readability and duplication reduction while preserving coverage strength
- targeted Design.md and canonical docs updates where ownership boundaries or stable behavior explanations need clarification

Non-goals:
- no runtime semantic changes
- no protocol or protobuf compatibility changes
- no .nbn / .nbs format changes
- no persistence or SQLite schema changes except comment/docs cleanup
- no edits to generated outputs under bin/, obj/, generated proto outputs, or rendered docs unless source files changed
- no repo-wide cosmetic churn divorced from maintainability value
- no file-splitting campaign for oversized files

Key repo-wide invariants and constraints:
- preserve input region 0 and output region 31 invariants
- preserve illegal IO-region axon protections
- preserve global compute-then-deliver tick sequencing
- preserve tick N visibility at compute N+1
- preserve snapshot-at-boundary semantics
- preserve reproduction protections and current request/response reason-code semantics
- preserve artifact store_uri resolution behavior, Workbench headless dispatcher behavior, and discovery-driven endpoint ownership

Dependency strategy / ordering principles:
- land repo baseline and enforcement first so later slices have a stable target
- clean up shared/contracts/artifacts/settings-discovery surfaces before the heaviest runtime slices
- keep runtime slices file-bounded where possible and avoid overlapping write ownership
- finish with test-harness/readability consolidation and a docs freshness sweep after subsystem work has stabilized

Intentional exclusions:
- generated artifacts and rendered docs without source changes
- behavior changes that would require spec, protocol, or persistence drift
- unrelated backlog items and existing tracker issues outside this refactor tree
design:
Major issue groups:
- baseline and enforcement
- shared/contracts/artifacts
- smaller runtime foundations (Brain, BrainHost, Observability, SettingsMonitor)
- major runtime subsystems (HiveMind, RegionHost, IO, WorkerNode, Reproduction, Speciation)
- tools and Workbench
- tests and final docs sweep

Cross-cutting risks to manage throughout the epic:
- actor cleanup must not perturb sender/remoting behavior, timeout handling, or message ordering
- logging cleanup must not change operator-visible failure codes or external contract semantics
- test dedup must not weaken assertion specificity or mask timing-sensitive failures
- XML-doc expansion must stay concise and avoid boilerplate restatement
- docs updates must capture only stable and reusable ownership/invariant information

Quirks that must remain intentionally preserved:
- IO-region and tick-barrier invariants
- snapshot boundary semantics
- reproduction protections
- artifact resolver semantics for non-file store_uri values
- Workbench inline dispatcher fallback when no Avalonia UI lifetime exists
- discovery-backed runtime endpoint ownership and operator surfaces
acceptance_criteria:
- Every child issue in this epic is complete and any Beads dependency links are satisfied.
- Handwritten code across src/, tools/, and tests/ follows the repo baseline established in this epic.
- Public and protected APIs touched by the refactor have concise XML documentation suitable for IDE/API use.
- Dead code, placeholder types, stale branches, misleading comments, and unused members/usings/constants targeted by the child issues are removed.
- Relevant project Design.md files are updated where stable responsibilities, ownership boundaries, or invariants were clarified.
- Canonical docs are updated only where stable behavior explanations or public contracts needed clarification, and docs freshness passes when source docs changed.
- dotnet build -c Release --disable-build-servers and dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers pass for the final refactor branch.
- The resulting issue set leaves no major audited maintainability work from this refactor scope unplanned.
close_reason: All child issues completed by the NBNv2-ogh harness.

title: Establish repo-wide maintainability baseline and enforcement
description:
Why this issue exists:
The repo currently has nullable and implicit usings enabled, but there is no root .editorconfig, no build-enforced style/documentation policy, and no shared maintainability baseline that later cleanup slices can target consistently.

Goals:
- add a repo-root coding-style baseline and MSBuild policy that later slices can rely on
- define pragmatic enforcement for naming, modifier ordering, guard clauses, nullability hygiene, async/cancellation conventions, and XML documentation expectations
- keep the baseline focused on maintainability value rather than broad cosmetic churn

Non-goals:
- no semantic changes to runtime or tool behavior
- no analyzer policy that forces mass rewrites outside the scope of later issues
- no generated-file enforcement or churn under bin/, obj/, generated proto outputs, or rendered docs
design:
Files expected to be involved:
- Directory.Build.props
- Directory.Build.targets
- .editorconfig
- NBNv2.sln
- selected *.csproj files under src/, tools/, and tests/ where opt-in or opt-out is required

Key symbols/components/subsystems likely to be touched:
- shared MSBuild properties
- repo-wide code style and documentation settings
- project-level overrides for test/UI/tool exceptions where justified

Constraints and invariants that must be preserved:

title: Implement INDEX include-render pipeline to generate docs/NBNv2.md on commit/push
description:
## Summary
Implement deterministic docs rendering from `docs/INDEX.md` into `docs/NBNv2.md` on commit/push, using include-marker expansion and CI validation.

## Precondition
Start only after the split-doc migration issue is complete.

## Why this is required
- `docs/INDEX.md` is the canonical template and includes split docs from `docs/sections/*` plus project-root design docs.
- `docs/NBNv2.md` must remain automatically synchronized for readers and agents.

## Scope
- Parse include markers in `docs/INDEX.md`:
  - `<!-- NBN:INCLUDE path=relative/path.md -->`
- Replace markers with referenced file content in deterministic order.
- Emit final output at `docs/NBNv2.md`.
- Add automation that validates generated output on push and pull_request.

## Required implementation details
1. Add cross-platform render scripts:
   - `tools/docs/render-nbnv2-docs.ps1`
   - `tools/docs/render-nbnv2-docs.sh`
2. Renderer rules:
   - repository-root relative path resolution
   - hard failure on missing files or invalid markers
   - deterministic ordering aligned with template/manifest
   - normalized line endings for stable diffs
3. Add GitHub Actions workflow for render and freshness validation on push/PR.
4. Add local-runner guidance:
   - self-hosted runner and/or `act`

5. Enforce one freshness policy:

6. Update guidance docs (`AGENTS.md`, `docs/manifest/NBNv2-DocumentMap.md`) with exact command/policy details, including canonical paths:

## Verification checklist
- CI passes on Windows and Linux.

- Broken include path causes deterministic actionable failure.
- Template/include edits are reflected in generated `docs/NBNv2.md`.

## Out of scope
- Content split/migration itself (tracked in predecessor issue).
- README rewrite (tracked separately).

title: Implement NBN installer and GitHub release pipeline
description:
Goal
Implement the NBN installer and GitHub release system defined in tools/dist/Design.md.

Canonical detail
- Canonical design doc: tools/dist/Design.md
- Canonical Workbench launch invariant: tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/Design.md

Decided plan
- Releases are manual and tag-triggered, not push-triggered.
- The release entrypoint is a local repo script that validates, computes the next version, creates the tag, and pushes it.
- GitHub Actions builds and publishes the release from the pushed tag.
- Windows installers use Inno Setup.
- Linux ships both .deb packages and distro-agnostic self-extracting installers.
- Worker-only also ships portable archives for Windows and Linux.
- Versioning is 2.<minor>.<patch>, with major fixed to 2 in this repo, minor changed manually in release/version.json, and patch computed automatically from existing tags for the current 2.<minor>.x line.
- Workbench must support two launch modes: local source-checkout mode and installed-binary mode. Installed-binary mode must never build source projects or call dotnet run.

Current evidence motivating this feature
- Workbench local launch is still source-tree oriented: tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/Services/LocalProjectLaunchPreparer.cs builds Release outputs and can fall back to dotnet run --project.
- Workbench currently manages runtime start flows from Orchestrator Start / Start All: tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/ViewModels/OrchestratorPanelViewModel.cs.
- The only existing dist helper is worker-only publishing: tools/dist/publish_worker_node.ps1 and tools/dist/publish_worker_node.sh.
- Current GitHub automation only validates docs freshness: .github/workflows/docs-render.yml.
- The product spec already requires cross-platform execution and worker-only processes: docs/NBNv2.md.

Required release artifacts per version
1. nbn-suite-<version>-win-x64-setup.exe
2. nbn-worker-<version>-win-x64-setup.exe
3. nbn-suite_<version>_amd64.deb
4. nbn-worker_<version>_amd64.deb
5. nbn-suite-<version>-linux-x64-installer.run
6. nbn-worker-<version>-linux-x64-installer.run
7. nbn-worker-<version>-win-x64-portable.zip
8. nbn-worker-<version>-linux-x64-portable.tar.gz

Required alias contract
- Full suite: nbn-workbench, nbn-settings, nbn-hivemind, nbn-io, nbn-repro, nbn-speciation, nbn-observability, nbn-worker, nbn-brainhost, nbn-regionhost, nbn-demohost, nbn-evolution-sim, nbn-perf-probe.
- Worker-only: nbn-worker.
- Installers put one bin directory on PATH. Portable worker archives do not modify PATH.

Implementation order
1. Add Workbench local-vs-installed launch mode abstraction.
2. Detect local source-checkout mode by repo markers relative to the running Workbench binary.
3. Keep current Release-build plus freshly-built-executable flow for local mode, including local-only dotnet run --no-build fallback.
4. Add installed-binary mode that resolves services/tools from a shipped runtime-manifest.json and falls back to PATH aliases only if the manifest is unavailable.
5. Generalize publishing from worker-only to all shipped executables for win-x64 and linux-x64.
6. Add release metadata files under release/, including release/version.json.
7. Add Windows Inno Setup packaging for full-suite and worker-only bundles.
8. Add Linux .deb packaging for full-suite and worker-only bundles.
9. Add Linux distro-agnostic .run packaging for full-suite and worker-only bundles.
10. Add worker-only portable zip/tar.gz packaging.
11. Add tag-triggered GitHub Actions release workflow that validates, builds, packages, and publishes the GitHub Release from the tag.
12. Add local release entry scripts for Windows and Linux.
13. Add AGENTS.md release instructions so agents know the release flow is manual, tag-triggered, and requires explicit human confirmation before execution.
14. Add smoke verification for installed-mode Workbench and packaged worker launch paths.

Notes on Workbench behavior
- Installed Workbench must use installed sibling binaries or PATH-resolved installed commands only.
- Installed Workbench must never require the .NET SDK or a source checkout.
- SettingsMonitor remains the only required bootstrap coordinate for the managed runtime startup flow.

External implementation references
- .NET deployment overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/deploying/
- .NET single-file deployment: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/deploying/single-file/overview
- GitHub workflow trigger docs: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/events-that-trigger-workflows
- GitHub CLI release creation: https://cli.github.com/manual/gh_release_create
- Inno Setup compiler CLI: https://jrsoftware.org/ishelp/index.php?topic=compilercmdline
acceptance_criteria: 1) The release/install/versioning plan in tools/dist/Design.md is implemented without introducing an automatic every-push release path. 2) Each release publishes the eight decided assets: Windows full installer, Windows worker installer, Linux full .deb, Linux worker .deb, Linux full .run, Linux worker .run, Windows worker portable zip, and Linux worker portable tar.gz. 3) Workbench distinguishes local source-checkout mode from installed-binary mode, keeps the current Release-build plus local-only dotnet run fallback in local mode, and never builds source projects or calls dotnet run in installed mode. 4) Installed Workbench resolves managed runtime launches from a shipped runtime-manifest.json with PATH-alias fallback and can Start/Start All without requiring a source checkout or .NET SDK. 5) Versioning follows 2.<minor>.<patch> with major fixed to 2 in this repo, manual minor changes via release/version.json, and automatic patch increment from existing tags for the active minor line. 6) GitHub Actions builds releases from pushed tags and creates the GitHub Release with the decided assets. 7) Windows packaging uses Inno Setup and Linux ships both .deb and distro-agnostic installer forms. 8) AGENTS.md and README.md explain the manual release flow, explicit confirmation requirement, how to install the shipped artifacts, how to use the installed commands and aliases, and the supported install/run paths.
close_reason: Implemented the manual tag-triggered release/install pipeline, release metadata, packaging automation, docs, and verification for the current repo executable inventory.

title: Add Workbench installed-vs-local runtime launch resolution
description: Implement the decided Workbench launch split under NBNv2-rcm: detect local source-checkout execution vs installed-binary execution, keep Release-build plus local-only dotnet run fallback in local mode, and resolve installed sibling binaries/PATH commands in installed mode without requiring source projects or the .NET SDK.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

LocalProjectLaunchPreparer now preserves the existing build-first local source-checkout flow, but when Workbench is not running from a detected repo checkout it resolves installed commands from an optional runtime-manifest.json and then PATH aliases, without calling dotnet build or dotnet run. RepoLocator now detects repo roots via repo markers and can locate runtime-manifest.json above the running Workbench base directory. Orchestrator and Speciation launch paths no longer hard-fail just because the repo root is absent; they delegate mode selection to the launch preparer. Added LocalProjectLaunchPreparer installed-mode tests plus compatibility updates in the Workbench view-model test fakes. comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Landed a diagnostics/automation slice instead of another speculative runtime patch. Workbench runtime-diagnostics mode now propagates explicit tick/delivery/input flags (including a new NBN_RUNTIME_TICK_DIAGNOSTICS_ENABLED switch), BrainSignalRouter and RegionShard emit first-epoch tick-chain logs under that switch, and tools/dev/pull-windows-build-test.sh now lets us git pull/build/test the Windows laptop repo directly over SSH. Remaining work: rerun the laptop local-only scenario with NBN_WORKBENCH_RUNTIME_DIAGNOSTICS_ENABLED=1, pull the richer logs directly, and fix the actual Windows-local tick stall from that evidence.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Pulled the first laptop run with explicit tick diagnostics. The new logs showed HiveMind dispatching TickCompute to the worker-hosted BrainRoot while BrainSignalRouter kept receiving routing updates and never logged TickCompute dispatch, and RegionShard never logged TickCompute recv. Landed a focused fix to prefer the registered SignalRouter over BrainRoot for compute/deliver dispatch in HiveMind, plus a regression in HiveMindTickBarrierTests that uses a brain-root actor which registers successfully but drops tick messages; the test only passes when HiveMind targets the router. Full suite is now 1048 passing on Linux.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Pulled the next laptop run and narrowed the post-fix failure from placement to input-drain liveness. Fresh logs show the first brain eventually ticking normally, then IO hits ; after that BrainSignalRouter expires the pending drain and had been clearing its cached IO gateway, causing subsequent ticks to run in skip-drain mode with no way to recover until another re-registration. Landed a focused router fix to keep the cached IO gateway through expired drains, plus BrainSignalRouterInputDrainTests coverage proving tick 2 still issues a drain request after tick 1 expires. Full suite is now 1049 passing on Linux.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Follow-up after the router fix: the remote Windows full suite exposed an environment-sensitive resolver test. Made ServiceEndpointResolverTests deterministic by using a loopback local candidate instead of a machine-specific LAN IP so Windows and Linux agree on local preference behavior.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Built a deterministic repro for the remaining tick stall in tests and fixed the runtime path. Added RemoteDescriptorTransportTests.WorkerNodeRemote_Transports_TickCompute_To_WorkerHosted_SignalRouter, which reproduces worker-hosted signal-router ticks across remoting. The failing path turned out to be shard completion forwarding, not raw router delivery: remote TickCompute reached the worker-hosted shard and compute executed, but TickComputeDone could disappear on the configured tick-sink path. Fixed RegionShardActor to send compute-done to the configured tick sink and a distinct fallback path (request sender or router), and tightened BrainSignalRouter first-snapshot same-process route localization so same-machine shard routes can stay local immediately. Verified with targeted Brain/transport tests and dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers (1050 passing). Remaining step is runtime confirmation on the Windows laptop local-only scenario; keeping the issue open until that retest is green.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Pulled the latest laptop logs after the post-73f2b3b run. That run never reached the tick path; HiveMind only logged placement requests, and the worker never showed any runtime-hosting output. The practical issue is that a Workbench-launched local worker can still come up before its HiveMind/IO discovery has settled, yet SettingsMonitor marks it ready based only on capabilities, so HiveMind can plan onto a worker that still lacks the endpoints it needs for brain metadata/bootstrap. Landed a mitigation slice: Workbench now seeds the locally launched worker with explicit HiveMind/IO endpoint PIDs via environment variables, WorkerNode seeds those registrations before discovery bootstrap, and Start All now launches IO before WorkerNode. Added a Workbench test covering the seed env population. Full suite is now 1051 passing locally. Keeping the issue open until the laptop local-only runtime run is confirmed green.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Pulled the next laptop run after the seeded-worker/start-order change. Placement is no longer the main blocker; the run reached steady compute/deliver for multiple brains, but the logs showed a new secondary problem from the previous compute-done fallback: RegionShard was sending TickComputeDone to both HiveMind and the router on every tick, HiveMind was ignoring the duplicate worker-root forwards as untracked payloads, and that extra completion traffic correlated with the much slower cadence under multiple brains. Tightened the fallback so sender fallback still works for direct request/reply tests, router fallback is only used on a shard's first successful completion, and steady-state compute-done goes to the configured tick sink only. Full suite remains 1051 passing locally. Keeping the issue open for one more laptop runtime confirmation.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: User requested a full rollback to the pre-mixed-topology baseline and to preserve only the Windows laptop automation tooling. This issue is being retired with the rollback rather than fixed forward on the mixed-topology branch state.

title: Clarify artifact manifest dedup semantics when identical bytes are stored with different metadata
description: While implementing NBNv2-zwv.31, review found that ArtifactStoreDatabase deduplicates on artifact_sha256 only. Re-storing identical bytes with a different media type or richer region index returns the earlier manifest metadata instead of reconciling it. Decide whether manifest metadata should merge, replace, or stay byte-identity keyed, then add explicit coverage and docs.
close_reason: Duplicate-byte artifact metadata is now deterministic: compatible region-index metadata merges, conflicting media-type/index changes reject, with regression coverage and docs updates.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Completed. Duplicate artifact handling remains artifact_sha256-keyed, but metadata is now deterministic: same-byte stores with the same media type may enrich missing/non-conflicting region-index metadata on the existing row, while conflicting media-type or conflicting region-index changes are rejected explicitly. Added reconciliation and cache-refresh coverage, updated docs/spec text, and validated through focused artifact tests plus a clean 847/847 full-suite run.

title: Handle request-style input when IO only has BrainRoot routing info
description: IoGateway now waits for request-style InputWrite/InputVector to be accepted by both the input coordinator and the brain router before acking the caller. That fixes the router-awareness race for normal signal-router routing, but ResolveRouterPidAsync can still fall back to BrainRootPid when SignalRouterPid is temporarily unavailable. BrainRootActor does not currently proxy request-style input messages or return IoCommandAck, so a request sent through that fallback can still time out or be dropped. Add explicit BrainRoot forwarding/ack behavior or tighten routing so request-style input never targets BrainRoot without a response path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Request-style InputWrite/InputVector do not time out or disappear when IO only has BrainRoot routing metadata. 2. Coverage proves request ack ordering remains correct through the fallback path. 3. Canonical IO docs stay accurate about request-style input ack semantics.

title: Orchestrator Endpoints: clarify Observability/Viz Hub endpoint and connection/launch semantics
description:
Capture and resolve operator-surface ambiguity in Orchestrator Endpoints/Local Launch for Observability and Viz Hub.

Scope:
- Endpoints card should explicitly expose the endpoint inputs needed for Observability/Viz usage (host/port with local defaults), including where Viz Hub endpoint ownership lives.
- Clarify and implement launch semantics: whether Observability/Viz is only started via Local Launch / Start All vs any implicit behavior.
- Clarify and implement connection semantics: 'Obs connected' should reflect actual service availability/reachability, not just static endpoint config at Workbench startup.
- Clarify naming/ownership in UX/docs (DebugHub/DebugServer terms, Observability responsibility boundaries, per-deployment vs per-Workbench-instance behavior).

Out of scope:
- Runtime visualization event correctness (tracked separately in visualization bug).

Added/updated Workbench tests: ConnectionViewModel defaults, Orchestrator Obs connected/offline mapping, WorkbenchClient observability reachability, plus full Release build/test pass (435 tests).

title: Add runtime stimulation path for non-input neurons/axons
description: Extend NBN IO/control protocol and runtime actors to allow targeted stimulation beyond InputWrite (region 0). Workbench visualizer should be able to send pulses to selected non-input neurons and selected axon routes, with invariant checks (no writes to disallowed output self-target paths) and clear failure statuses.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented runtime neuron pulse + runtime state-write protocol path (io proto -> IoGateway -> BrainSignalRouter -> RegionShard) and enabled selected-item pulse actions for non-input neurons and route neuron endpoints. Added router forwarding and Workbench interaction tests; release build + tests green.

title: Enforce IO neuron function constraints across designer flows
description: Apply explicit input/output neuron function restrictions for random creation, manual edits, import normalization, and export validation. Ensure invalid combinations are prevented and clearly surfaced.
close_reason: Closed

title: Approximate plasticity nudges for useful adaptation (no full training algorithm)
description:
Reproduction remains the primary adaptation/learning mechanism. This issue improves plasticity as an approximate local nudge system, not as a full training framework.

Scope:
- Refine bounded local plasticity behavior (Hebbian-like correlation/anti-correlation tendencies plus stabilization/homeostatic nudges).
- Optionally incorporate weak, local predictive-coding-inspired heuristics as nudges only.
- Encourage (not force) input-dependent outputs, short-horizon anticipation tendencies, and memory-supportive/stable subspace formation.
- Ensure viability across input cadences (every tick, periodic, bursty, irregular); nothing may require per-tick input to function.

Explicit non-goals:
- No backpropagation.
- No canonical layered predictive-coding training architecture.
- No large centralized training loop.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Docs/spec text defines the approximate/nudge nature of the rule set and explicit non-goals.
2) Runtime supports bounded local plasticity terms with conservative defaults and deterministic behavior.
3) Tests demonstrate improved input-output dependency and short-memory/prediction-proxy behavior (when enabled) versus control baselines.
4) Behavior is validated across multiple input cadence patterns (regular and irregular).
5) No regressions in invariants, determinism guarantees, or snapshot/recovery correctness.
close_reason: Completed: bounded deterministic local plasticity nudges implemented with full cadence/determinism regression coverage and docs updates.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
﻿NBNv2-zwv.8 is now implemented and provides the baseline plasticity contract xr4 should build on.

New baseline semantics:
- `plasticity_rate` controls probabilistic gate probability scaling.
- `plasticity_delta` controls update magnitude step.

- Apply nudge math as bounded modulation of effective rate/delta (or equivalent local term) without breaking baseline invariants.
- Keep disabled-mode behavior equivalent to zwv.8 baseline.

title: Define UUID byte order for NBN artifacts
description: The .nbs header stores brain_id_uuid as 16 raw bytes and .proto Uuid uses bytes, but the canonical byte order isn't specified. Current C# IO uses Guid.ToByteArray()/new Guid(bytes), which is little-endian for the first fields. Decide and document the canonical UUID byte order for on-disk/on-wire formats and align IO code + tests accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: - NBNv2_Design.md specifies the UUID byte order for .nbn/.nbs and .proto Uuid bytes\n- NbnBinary read/write (and any other serializers) use the chosen order consistently\n- Tests cover byte-order roundtrips against a fixed UUID example
notes: Specified RFC 4122 byte order for UUID bytes; updated NbnBinary + tests.

title: Implement Real Rescheduling (No Simulated Delay)
description:
Replace HiveMind's current simulated reschedule path (`RescheduleSimulatedMs`) with actual shard placement actions: unassign/reassign (and split/merge where needed), routing-table updates, and deterministic resume at tick boundaries.

Expand rescheduling placement policy for mixed-latency environments:
- Prefer keeping each brain within a low-latency locality (same computer and especially same network) rather than splitting one brain's shards across higher-latency links.
- Prefer distributing different brains across computers/networks before splitting a single brain across computers/networks.
- Accept temporary free-resource imbalance when needed to preserve lower-latency placement.
- If constraints require it, still distribute a brain across devices/networks with different or longer inter-worker latency rather than blocking required rescheduling.
- Use average inter-worker ping as the main placement signal, not only worker-to-HiveMind ping.
acceptance_criteria:
1) `BeginReschedule/CompleteReschedule` execute real assignment plan updates instead of timer-only simulation.
2) Routing snapshots and shard output sinks are updated for moved shards before ticks resume.
3) Repeated reschedule requests obey existing min-tick/min-minute throttles without dropping required actions.
4) Placement scoring prefers low-latency brain locality, tolerates temporary capacity imbalance for that locality goal, and allows cross-device/network brain splitting when necessary.
5) HiveMind placement decisions use inter-worker latency telemetry (average ping between workers), not just worker-to-HiveMind latency.
close_reason: Implemented real rescheduling, inter-worker latency-aware placement, and regression coverage

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Follow-up implementation direction from issue expansion:
- Cross-network or cross-device shard placement should be treated as a necessary fallback, not the default.
- Latency awareness should be based on worker-to-worker measurements, since worker-to-HiveMind ping alone can hide shard-to-shard path cost.
- Preferred telemetry approach: HiveMind requests/collects specific inter-worker ping stats for scheduling decisions, instead of requiring continuous full-mesh worker self-pinging.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented real rescheduling for HiveMind/WorkerNode placement. Runtime now executes actual placement updates instead of timer-only simulation, retries queued reschedules when throttle windows clear, and waits for current-placement-epoch shard re-registration plus output-sink refresh before reschedule completion. Placement scoring now uses inter-worker RTT telemetry and prefers low-latency whole-brain locality before spreading a brain across slower worker boundaries. Updated canonical/runtime design docs and added regression coverage for worker peer RTT probes, placement inventory telemetry, planner locality behavior, queued retry throttling, and pending-reschedule completion. Validation: dotnet build NBNv2.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp; dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp (Passed 772/772).

title: Reflect externally applied settings in Workbench operator surfaces
description:
Ensure Workbench operator option surfaces reflect externally applied runtime settings (not just locally entered values), including reproduction defaults and tick-cadence-related settings where exposed.

Scope:
- When external systems change runtime/config values, Workbench panels should refresh to authoritative values.
- Repro panel defaults/options should reconcile with external/default policy updates rather than silently drifting.
- Cadence-related displays/controls should reflect authoritative runtime state and update live where supported.
- Distinguish authoritative state, local pending edits, and rejected/overridden values in UX.

This closes a control-surface alignment gap between external orchestrators and Workbench.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Workbench surfaces for relevant settings ingest and display externally applied authoritative values without reconnect requirements where protocols allow live updates.
2) Reproduction defaults/options in Workbench reflect externally applied policy/default changes and expose any source-of-truth conflicts clearly.
3) Tick-cadence-related state shown in Workbench tracks authoritative runtime values and updates deterministically.
4) Tests cover external-change -> Workbench-state synchronization, including conflict/pending-edit scenarios.
5) Docs/spec alignment notes are updated with authoritative-state and UX semantics.
close_reason: Completed: Workbench authoritative settings reflection now covers direct Settings DB value updates for existing rows, cadence status refreshes authoritative HiveMind state, regression coverage covers external-change sync/pending-edit cases, and docs were updated/rendered.
