title: Fix Output Vector Contract For Sharded Output Regions
description: IO output vector handling currently allows runtime output-width drift and silently drops mismatched vectors (OutputCoordinatorActor.EnsureOutputWidth/EmitVector), which breaks fixed output-width invariants and sharded output-vector semantics from the NBN spec.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Output width remains fixed to brain registration/definition metadata and is not mutated by observed events. 2) Output vector publication for sharded output regions produces the spec-aligned full brain-level output vector per tick with deterministic ordering. 3) Invalid/mismatched vector payloads are surfaced via deterministic telemetry/debug paths (not silent drops). 4) Tests cover single-shard and multi-shard output vector behavior and invariant enforcement. 5) Documentation is updated with exact output-vector semantics (including sharding behavior) for integrators/operators.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented fixed-width output vector contract in OutputCoordinatorActor, added shard-segment aggregation (full ordered brain-level vector per tick), added deterministic rejection telemetry (nbn.io.output.vector.rejected / nbn.io.output.single.rejected), added tests for single-shard + multi-shard + overlap rejection, and updated output-vector docs.

title: Drop Late SignalBatch Contributions After Tick Advancement
description: RegionShard currently records late SignalBatch telemetry but still applies late contributions to inbox state (RegionShardActor.HandleSignalBatch), which violates tick semantics that signal delivery for tick N is only for tick N+1 and late arrivals should not perturb later ticks.
acceptance_criteria: 1) When batch.TickId is less than last_compute_tick_id, RegionShard records late telemetry/debug and does not apply contributions. 2) Late-batch handling remains idempotent and does not regress ack behavior or routing accounting. 3) Tests cover late-batch drop behavior and verify no buffer mutation from dropped batches.
close_reason: Implemented and validated

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented late SignalBatch drop in RegionShardActor.HandleSignalBatch: late batches now record signal.late telemetry/debug and skip ApplyContribution while preserving existing SignalBatchAck flow. Added RegionShardDebugEmissionTests coverage for (1) late batch drop + ack and (2) repeated late idempotence + ack count. Validation: dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --filter FullyQualifiedName~RegionShardDebugEmissionTests (4/4 pass) and full dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --no-build (333/333 pass).

title: Fix spawn regression where brain pauses then disappears after successful spawn
description:
Regression after recent WIP changes: in Workbench Designer, random brain spawn now returns success and a BrainId, briefly appears in Visualizer Known Brains as Paused, then disappears.

Current run evidence (2026-02-26 UTC):
- BrainId: 3bb52932-83fb-4600-9c2f-1eeee7345236
- HiveMind logs show placement accepted (workers=1), then repeated TickCompute/TickDeliver timeouts, then global pause:
  - TickCompute timeout tick 275 pending=1
  - TickDeliver timeout tick 275 pendingBrains=3bb5...
  - repeated through ticks 276-280
  - Paused all brains: tick 280 timed out (pause after 6 timeouts)
- WorkerNode log has only startup lines (no shard/compute completion logging), suggesting either compute completion acks are not emitted, not correlated, or being dropped/ignored upstream.

Already implemented in this session:
1) Removed Designer post-spawn explicit placement request to avoid double-placement churn.
   - tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/ViewModels/DesignerPanelViewModel.cs
2) Settings DB default path moved to per-machine LocalApplicationData with CLI override retained.
   - src/Nbn.Runtime.SettingsMonitor/SettingsMonitorOptions.cs
   - tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/ViewModels/ConnectionViewModel.cs
   - tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/ViewModels/OrchestratorPanelViewModel.cs
   - Linux included via Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData fallback semantics.
3) Tests updated/added for the above behavior changes.

Current hypothesis:
- Spawn/placement now succeeds, but tick completion barrier accounting is not receiving or not matching RegionShard completions for newly assigned shards, causing deterministic timeout -> pause.
- Secondary symptom: Known Brains list eventually drops the paused brain, potentially due to unregister/state sync gap after timeout-driven pause/failure path.

Next debug/implementation plan:
1) Trace TickComputeDone/TickDeliverDone send path from RegionShard/WorkerNode to HiveMind and verify sender/request semantics and IDs (brain/region/shard/tick).
2) Audit HiveMind pending barrier keying and matching logic during mid-compute shard registration transitions.
3) Add targeted logging around pending set mutations and done-message matching decisions.
4) Patch matching/registration race and add focused tests for spawn + first ticks + no false timeout under single-worker local launch.
5) Validate from Workbench end-to-end: spawned brain stays present and does not auto-pause/disappear.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Fresh local launch: Designer random brain spawn remains present in Known Brains for at least 60s without automatic pause/disappearance.
2) HiveMind no longer times out ticks solely due to newly registered shards during normal spawn path.
3) New/updated tests cover the fixed barrier/ack matching behavior and fail on the pre-fix regression.
4) Workbench/Settings default DB behavior remains LocalApplicationData-based (Windows/Linux) with explicit --db override preserved.
close_reason: Spawn regression resolved; remaining visualizer/reproduction problems tracked separately in NBNv2-bje and NBNv2-776.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Update (2026-02-26):
- Implemented runtime fix in HiveMind tick barrier sender validation:
  - TickComputeDone and TickDeliverDone now accept either:
    1) expected tracked sender (existing strict path), or
    2) trusted brain controller sender (BrainRoot/SignalRouter) as fallback.
- Rationale: in some startup/discovery/orderings, completion messages can be forwarded via controller path, and strict shard-only sender match causes false pending barriers -> compute/deliver timeouts -> global pause.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Compaction checkpoint (2026-02-26, post-regression triage)

Latest confirmed code changes beyond prior comment:
1) WorkerNode tick completion forwarding
- File: src/Nbn.Runtime.WorkerNode/WorkerNodeActor.cs
- Added handlers/forwarding for TickComputeDone and TickDeliverDone, using HiveMind endpoint hint fallback.
- Test: tests/Nbn.Tests/WorkerNode/WorkerNodeDiscoveryAndPlacementTests.cs
  - TickCompletionForwarding_UsesHiveMindHint_WhenEndpointMissing

2) HiveMind dispatch gating while placement execution is incomplete
- File: src/Nbn.Runtime.HiveMind/HiveMindActor.cs
- Added CanDispatchTickToBrain(...) gating in compute/deliver dispatch loops.
- Brains with non-zero placement epoch are skipped until placement execution completes.
- Updated lifecycle tests accordingly.

3) WorkerNode observed PID reconciliation fix (critical)
- File: src/Nbn.Runtime.WorkerNode/WorkerNodeActor.cs
- BuildObservedActorPid(...) now prefers HostedPid (normalized) over configured actor name.
- Added NormalizeObservedActorId(...) to strip anonymous $... path segments while preserving meaningful path parts.
- Fixed wrong controller PID path in reconcile observations:
  - before: host/brain-...-root
  - after:  host/worker-node-1/brain-...-root

4) HiveMind tick barrier instrumentation
- File: src/Nbn.Runtime.HiveMind/HiveMindActor.cs
- Added env-gated logs: NBN_HIVEMIND_LOG_TICK_BARRIER=1
- Logs include compute dispatch targets and recv/ignored done events.

5) Settings DB default placement migration (cross-platform)
- Files:
  - src/Nbn.Runtime.SettingsMonitor/SettingsMonitorOptions.cs
  - tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/ViewModels/ConnectionViewModel.cs
  - tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/ViewModels/OrchestratorPanelViewModel.cs
- Default now resolves to LocalApplicationData-style per-machine location; --db override retained; local launch does not force explicit default path.

Runtime validation outcome after above fixes:
- tools/demo/run_local_hivemind_demo.ps1 progressed from registration_timeout to successful registration.
- IO scenario returned successful energy/plasticity command acks.
- Barrier logs showed valid compute dispatch + compute done for all 3 shards.

Remaining failure (current blocker):
- TickDeliverDone eventually stops arriving; HiveMind logs repeated TickDeliver timeouts and then pauses brains after timeout budget.
- BrainSignalRouter trace indicates many ticks with expectedAcks=0, then repeated:
  - Pending input drains expired before tick=... expired=...
- Working hypothesis: BrainSignalRouter input-drain pending lifecycle (_pendingInputDrains / DrainInputs->InputDrain path) can wedge deliver completion for later ticks.

Next immediate debug slice (resume plan):
1) Deep trace in BrainSignalRouterActor around:
   - HandleTickDeliver
   - ProcessTickDeliver
   - HandleInputDrain
   - ExpirePendingInputDrains
2) Prove/patch behavior when ShouldDrainInputs() is true and InputDrain is late/missing:
   - each tick must deterministically complete/fail in a way HiveMind can advance (no silent starvation of TickDeliverDone).
3) Add focused regression tests:
   - missing/late InputDrain
   - expectedAcks=0 path
   - repeated ticks after drain expiry
   - ensure no indefinite pending brain at HiveMind barrier.

Operational note:
- Main issue now appears deterministic logic-wedge, not simple timeout tuning.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Update (2026-02-26, drain-wedge mitigation pass)

Implemented additional runtime mitigation for repeated deliver timeouts when input drain replies stop arriving:

Code change:
- src/Nbn.Runtime.Brain/BrainSignalRouterActor.cs
  - In ExpirePendingInputDrains(...), after expiring stale drain state, router now clears cached `_ioGatewayPid`.
  - Rationale: repeated drain-expiry indicates stale/unreachable IO gateway PID; clearing it prevents the router from re-entering the same drain wait loop every tick.
  - Resulting behavior: first affected tick may still timeout upstream, but subsequent ticks proceed without repeated drain waits until IO gateway is re-captured via sender or RegisterIoGateway.
  - Log now includes `ioGatewayCleared=true` on drain-expiry events.

Regression test added:

title: Default-on probabilistic quantized homeostasis decay for neuron buffers (optional energy coupling)
description:
Add a first-class homeostasis/decay path for neuron buffers that is enabled by default and quantization-aware. This addresses the current runtime quirk where reset-based decay behavior may not run for low-activity neurons because reset is post pre-activation-gate.

Design intent:
- Preserve existing merge/activation/reset semantics while adding a distinct idle homeostasis path.
- Homeostasis defaults should produce gradual fading-memory behavior without requiring per-neuron reset-function specialization.
- Use probabilistic quantized movement as the default update method (per operator direction).

Proposed runtime policy surface:
- homeostasis_decay_enabled (default true)
- homeostasis_target_mode (default zero; extensible for fixed-rest-value modes)
- homeostasis_update_mode = probabilistic_quantized_step (default)
- homeostasis_base_probability (bounded [0,1], conservative default)
- homeostasis_min_step_codes (default 1)
- homeostasis_energy_coupling_enabled (default false)
- homeostasis_energy_target_scale / homeostasis_energy_probability_scale (bounded optional modulation knobs)

Ordering expectation:
- apply homeostasis after inbox merge and before pre-activation gating, so low-activity neurons still evolve
- keep reset behavior as post-activation logic (no double-counting reset as homeostasis)

Energy tie-in policy:
- optional only; disabled must preserve non-energy homeostasis behavior
- when energy surface unavailable/disabled, treat energy-normalization as neutral (no coupling effect)

Documentation expectation:
- update canonical docs/spec sections to define homeostasis semantics, defaults, ordering, and operator controls.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Runtime implements default-on homeostasis decay as a distinct path from reset, with deterministic tick-ordering: merge -> homeostasis -> pre-activation -> activation -> reset/fire. 2) Default update method uses probabilistic quantized movement toward target (not tiny float-only nudges), with at least one code-step movement when an update is applied. 3) Optional energy coupling is implemented with explicit bounds and disabled-by-default behavior; with coupling disabled, behavior matches non-energy homeostasis baseline. 4) Snapshot/reload behavior remains stable and deterministic with homeostasis enabled (no invariant regressions). 5) Tests cover: ordering semantics, low-activity neuron evolution, quantization-step correctness, probabilistic determinism under fixed seeds, and energy-coupled vs uncoupled behavior. 6) Workbench/operator surfaces and docs expose defaults and controls clearly, including recommended safe ranges and tradeoffs.
close_reason: Implemented and validated runtime/proto/io/workbench/docs homeostasis surface; full build/test/docs gates passed.

title: Introduce dedicated Speciation runtime service with persistent taxonomy epochs
description:
Introduce a dedicated control-plane runtime service (Nbn.Runtime.Speciation) that owns species assignment and lineage/taxonomy state.

Scope:
- Host a Speciation manager actor as a deterministic single-writer decision authority.
- Persist taxonomy state in SQLite (species, membership, lineage edges, decisions, config snapshots).
- Introduce epoch/version boundaries so reset starts a new epoch without deleting historical decisions.
- Keep reproduction compatibility gates/synthesis unchanged; consume existing brain/reproduction artifacts and metadata as inputs.
- On startup, load persisted state then reconcile currently known/registered brains for missing membership.
acceptance_criteria:
1) New runtime service process exists for speciation with remote config and health surface consistent with other core services.
2) Persistent schema stores species membership, lineage links, decision metadata, policy_version, and epoch_id.
3) Membership decisions are immutable within an epoch once committed.
4) Reset operation starts a new epoch/version without rewriting historical epochs.
5) Startup path reloads state and performs deterministic reconciliation for brains missing membership in current epoch.
close_reason: Completed: dedicated Speciation runtime service with persistent taxonomy epochs, immutable per-epoch membership, reset-to-new-epoch history preservation, deterministic startup reconciliation, and passing runtime/test/doc quality gates.

- Support both modes: online stateful calls and batch evaluation/commit over URIs/ArtifactRefs.
- Distinguish dry-run evaluation from commit/apply operations with explicit reason/error codes.
- Add IO routing/forwarding behavior and failure normalization consistent with other services.

1) New speciation proto schema and generated contracts are added and versioned in canonical protocol docs.
2) IO Gateway exposes request/response paths for stateful and batch speciation operations.
3) Batch APIs accept artifact references/URIs and support dry-run versus commit semantics.
4) Failure/abort reason codes are explicit, stable, and covered by contract tests.
5) Existing reproduction APIs remain backward compatible.

title: Implement Speciation assignment engine with lineage ingestion and sticky membership
description:
Implement the operational speciation decision pipeline that ingests lineage/reproduction signals and assigns stable species membership for new/known brains.

Scope:
- Ingest parent/child lineage edges and similarity metrics from runtime/reproduction outputs.
- Apply configurable decision policy with thresholds + hysteresis to reduce taxonomy churn.
- Record decision provenance (inputs used, policy version, scores, timestamp, epoch).
- Enforce no in-place reclassification within epoch; support explicit re-evaluation tooling into a new epoch.
- Provide deterministic ordering/serialization for concurrent assignment requests.
acceptance_criteria:
1) New brains can be assigned species deterministically from lineage + similarity evidence.
2) Decision policy supports configurable thresholds/hysteresis and emits clear decision reasons.
3) Decision provenance is persisted per assignment (policy_version, scores, source refs, epoch_id).
4) Membership is sticky within an epoch; reclassification requires explicit new-epoch workflow.
5) Concurrency tests confirm single-writer deterministic results under parallel request load.
close_reason: Completed: deterministic lineage-aware assignment engine with configurable thresholds+hysteresis, persisted provenance + lineage ingest, and concurrency-focused regression coverage.

Added store support for latest lineage-child lookup and lineage ingest within assignment transaction. Expanded coverage with new manager tests (lineage similarity assignment, hysteresis hold, deterministic derived-species behavior, parallel-load single-writer/sticky membership) and store tests (lineage-edge ingest + latest-child lookup). title: Build standalone evolution simulation tool for speciation/visualization stress runs
description:
Build a standalone evolution simulation tool (not a core runtime service) to generate multi-generation reproduction activity for speciation/visualization/testing workflows.

Scope and boundaries:
- Implement as a tool binary (e.g., under tools/), not as Nbn.Runtime.* core service.
- No new IO Gateway routing or IO-specific protocol awareness is required.
- Tool is invokable directly by operators/external systems (run binary/CLI and connect to existing runtime services as needed).

Functional behavior:
- Maintain a configurable parent pool from artifact refs/URIs (and optional transient in-memory registry of child definitions).
- Periodically attempt parent-pair compatibility and reproduction cycles.
- Prefer artifact-only reproduction flow by default (no spawn/run required).
- Default commit children to Speciation assignment enabled.
- Default spawn/run of children disabled, with an optional mode to spawn/run for separate test scenarios.

Diversity policy:
- Support multiple reproduction runs per compatible pair.
- Run count increases as compatibility decreases (still compatible), to encourage variety/speciation.
- Formula and caps must be configurable with deterministic seed behavior.

Operator integration:
- Expose start/stop/status/session controls suitable for Workbench integration from the Speciation pane.
- Stop must be clean/idempotent; periodic loop halts immediately when toggled off.
acceptance_criteria:
1) New tool binary exists outside core runtime services and can be executed independently.
2) Tool performs periodic compatibility+reproduction cycles from artifact refs/URIs without requiring spawned brains.
3) Default behavior is artifact-only with `commit_to_speciation=true` and `spawn_children=false`.
4) Inverse-compatibility multi-run policy is implemented and configurable (with deterministic seeds and hard safety caps).
5) Tool exposes start/stop/status/session controls consumable by Workbench and supports clean stop/resume semantics.
6) No IO Gateway contract changes are required for this tool.
7) Tests cover deterministic session replay, stop/start control semantics, and run-count behavior across compatibility bands.
notes: Reopened for follow-up scope: add Speciation pane epoch-history reset/delete controls, max-divergence monitor, and richer live simulator status telemetry; keep in-progress pending remaining dependencies.

title: Route Workbench artifact consumers through ArtifactStoreResolver and support non-file store_uri hydration
description:
Workbench currently emits and consumes artifact references, but some consumer paths still bypass the shared runtime artifact resolver and assume a local artifact-root directory. In particular, definition hydration in Workbench Viz probes local paths and opens LocalArtifactStore directly instead of honoring non-file store_uri registration, environment bootstrap, and node-local cache behavior.

Replace local-path-only reads in existing Workbench artifact consumers with ArtifactStoreResolver-backed reads. Preserve current local-file behavior for plain filesystem roots, but make non-file store_uri artifact refs work end-to-end anywhere Workbench already consumes runtime-exported artifact refs. Add regression coverage for registered non-file stores and environment-mapped stores.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Existing Workbench artifact-consumer paths use ArtifactStoreResolver/shared runtime semantics instead of direct LocalArtifactStore path probing when store_uri is non-file.
2) Workbench definition hydration works for local/file stores, registered non-file stores, and exact-match NBN_ARTIFACT_STORE_URI_MAP mappings.
3) Existing local artifact-root behavior remains unchanged for file-backed flows.
4) Regression tests cover successful local + non-file hydration and actionable failure behavior.
close_reason: Implemented resolver-backed Workbench artifact hydration with tests and design-doc update.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented Workbench Viz artifact hydration through ArtifactStoreResolver for non-file store_uri refs while preserving local artifact-root probing for file-backed flows. Added actionable resolver-failure status instead of collapsing non-file resolution errors into generic root misses. Added regression coverage for local store roots, registered non-file stores, exact NBN_ARTIFACT_STORE_URI_MAP mappings, and unregistered non-file failure messaging. Updated tools/Nbn.Tools.Workbench/Design.md to document the shared resolver behavior. Validation: dotnet test tests/Nbn.Tests/Nbn.Tests.csproj -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp --filter FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.Workbench.VizPanelViewModelInteractionTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.RuntimeArtifacts.ArtifactStoreResolverTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nbn.Tests.RuntimeArtifacts.ArtifactStorePartialReadTests (64/64).

title: Add a concrete remote artifact-store backend for distributed deployments
description:
The runtime artifact subsystem now supports non-file store_uri resolution through a pluggable adapter path, but the repository still lacks an in-tree concrete remote/distributed artifact-store backend beyond local stores and test doubles. Distributed deployments need a real adapter implementation that can serve manifests, content-addressed full reads, range reads, and writes over a non-file transport or remote backing store.

Add at least one concrete production-grade remote artifact-store adapter/client implementation in-tree and wire it through the existing registry/resolver path. Document bootstrap/registration and failure semantics for operators, and verify runtime callers can use it without local-path assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
1) The repo contains a concrete non-file artifact-store implementation used outside tests/demo wrappers.
2) The implementation supports manifest lookup, full artifact reads, range reads, and artifact writes while preserving content-addressed semantics.
3) HiveMind, RegionHost/WorkerNode, and Reproduction can operate against it through the existing resolver/registry path.
4) Canonical docs and operator guidance explain registration/bootstrap, cache interaction, and failure modes for distributed deployments.
5) Integration coverage exercises the adapter through real runtime flows, not only unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented built-in HTTP artifact-store backend, runtime-flow coverage, and docs updates.

Completed .29 by adding a built-in HTTP(S) artifact-store backend through ArtifactStoreRegistry and ArtifactStoreResolver, plus runtime-flow coverage across WorkerNode, HiveMind snapshot persistence, Reproduction child exports, resolver and Workbench HTTP hydration, and dedicated HTTP backend conformance tests. Updated canonical and design docs for bootstrap, cache, range-read fallback, and failure semantics. Validation passed: targeted runtime and HTTP suites 153/153; artifact suites 23/23; docs render and freshness check passed.

title: Add Backpressure Pause Priority Strategies
description: Backpressure currently pauses all brains. Implement configured pause strategy selection (oldest-first, newest-first, lowest energy, lowest priority, externally ordered list) per spec section 6.5.
acceptance_criteria: 1) New config surface selects pause strategy. 2) Pause decision targets selected subset/order instead of unconditional PauseAllBrains. 3) Tests verify each strategy deterministically chooses expected brain(s).

HiveMind now selects a targeted brain to pause instead of pausing all brains, with configurable strategy selection (oldest-first, newest-first, lowest-energy, lowest-priority, external-order), external-order list parsing via HiveMind options, and per-brain pause priority carried on SpawnBrain/RegisterBrain. title: Add full Workbench artifact-store round-trip flows for definitions and snapshots
description:
Workbench can already write local artifacts for designer-spawn and artifact-based reproduction requests, but the operator surface is still file-centric for export/import and restore. There is no complete Workbench round-trip for saving generated definitions/snapshots directly to an artifact store, surfacing the resulting ArtifactRef/store_uri, and later reopening or restoring from that artifact reference without detouring through files.

Add explicit artifact-store-first Workbench workflows for definitions and snapshots. Reuse the shared artifact resolver semantics, support both local/file and non-file stores, and surface actionable artifact identifiers to the operator.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Workbench can save/export supported .nbn and .nbs artifacts directly into an artifact store and show the resulting ArtifactRef/store_uri details.
2) Workbench can load or restore supported artifacts from an ArtifactRef (or equivalent sha + store_uri input) without requiring the bytes to first exist as a local file export.
3) Flows work for local/file stores and non-file stores reachable through the shared resolver path.
4) UI/service tests cover success cases, validation failures, and store-resolution errors.
5) Canonical docs or nearby design docs describe the operator workflow and limits.
close_reason: Implemented and verified Workbench artifact-store round-trip flows.

comments.author: Hailey Collet

Added Workbench design-doc coverage and Designer regression tests for non-file success, local snapshot round-trip, invalid sha validation, and unresolved-store errors. title: Add artifact dedup/compression/ref-count conformance coverage and maintenance notes
description:
The artifact store clearly implements chunk-level dedup bookkeeping, chunk compression metadata, and reference counts, but the current coverage mostly exercises lookup/fetch flows rather than proving cross-artifact chunk reuse and documenting operational semantics. The remaining gap is confidence: we need deterministic tests and design notes that demonstrate dedup actually occurs, compression metadata round-trips correctly, and current retention/cleanup behavior is explicit.

Add focused artifact-store conformance coverage for chunk reuse, compression metadata, and ref-count accounting. If retention/GC remains unsupported, document that explicitly instead of leaving the maintenance story implicit.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Tests store overlapping artifacts and verify chunk payload reuse rather than duplicate chunk writes.
2) Tests verify chunk metadata, compression labels/stored lengths, and ref_count increments for reused chunks.
3) Any bookkeeping defects found are fixed in runtime code rather than normalized in tests.
4) Runtime/design docs explicitly describe current retention/cleanup behavior; if GC/delete is not implemented, that limitation is stated clearly.
close_reason: Added artifact-store conformance coverage for chunk reuse/compression/ref-count bookkeeping, documented current append-only retention limits, regenerated canonical docs, and logged explicit follow-up issues for adjacent manifest/concurrency validation gaps.

Updated canonical artifact-storage docs plus Nbn.Runtime.Artifacts/Design.md to state current append-only retention semantics and manual cleanup requirements; regenerated docs/NBNv2.md. title: Implement ILGPU RegionShard Backend + CPU Parity
description: Add optional ILGPU compute backend (kernel-per-function model) with runtime selection/fallback to CPU and parity validation versus CPU backend per spec section 10.2.
acceptance_criteria: 1) GPU backend path exists with lazy kernel compilation/caching and explicit fallback. 2) Parity tests compare CPU vs GPU outputs/cost fields across representative functions. 3) Placement heuristics can prefer GPU-capable nodes when enabled.
close_reason: Implemented ILGPU RegionShard backend with explicit CPU fallback, parity coverage, placement preference gating, Workbench GPU capability chips, full-suite validation, and updated docs.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Reviewed epic/root issue and completed required scout pass before implementation. Evidence so far: (1) RegionShard execution is still CPU-only; there is no backend-selection/fallback surface in RegionShardActorConfig/RegionShardActor/WorkerNode hosting. (2) Worker capability probing already publishes GPU/VRAM/ILGPU telemetry and GPU score, and HiveMind placement already prefers GPU-capable workers heuristically for large compute shards, but that preference is not yet tied to actual backend enablement/availability. (3) Spec requires optional ILGPU backend with lazy kernel compilation/caching, explicit CPU fallback, and parity against CPU. (4) Runtime invariants to preserve: do not send TickComputeDone before device work/outbox/output visibility is synchronized; preserve late-batch handling, snapshot correctness, and IO-region invariants. (5) Current tests cover CPU RegionShard behavior, placement heuristics, and capability probing, but there is no ILGPU-vs-CPU parity coverage or GPU-availability-aware runtime/perf execution coverage yet. Next steps: add backend selection + explicit fallback in RegionHost/WorkerNode, implement ILGPU backend path (CUDA-first, OpenCL fallback if viable), tighten placement preference gating, then add parity/perf coverage with GPU-presence-aware skips when no compatible accelerator is available.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text:
Implemented and validated the RegionShard ILGPU backend pass for supported shard/runtime shapes with explicit CPU fallback, plus placement gating and Workbench worker-capability chips.

Delivered:
- Added shared NBN_REGIONSHARD_BACKEND=auto|cpu|gpu preference parsing and threaded it through RegionHost/HiveMind/WorkerNode.
- RegionHost now dispatches through a backend selector with lazy ILGPU initialization, CUDA-first device selection, per-function kernel caching, and explicit CPU fallback when runtime features/function IDs are unsupported.
- Worker-hosted auto selection is capability-first (scaled by allowed resource-use percentages), not blindly GPU-first; heavy compute shards choose the stronger backend on that worker.
- HiveMind placement tests now cover ComputeBackendPreference gating, so GPU preference is suppressed when backend preference is forced to CPU and still preferred when allowed.
- Added RegionHost parity coverage for CPU vs GPU outputs/costs, actor snapshot parity after GPU compute, and a GPU-availability-aware high-load benchmark. On this RTX 5090 host, the benchmark case measured 262,144 neurons with fan-out 2 at about 219.622 ms CPU vs 83.042 ms GPU over the benchmark window.
- Workbench > Orchestrator worker-host chips now surface capability info through a second chip (for example CUDA 90, CPU, etc.) based on worker inventory capabilities.
- Updated canonical/runtime docs and regenerated docs/NBNv2.md.

Validation:
- Targeted GPU parity / snapshot / benchmark tests passed.
- Targeted RegionHost + placement + tick-barrier/router tests passed (99/99).
- Orchestrator panel tests passed (53/53).
- Full suite passed: 945/945.
- Docs render + freshness check passed.

Follow-up / residual note:
- This pass is shard-level capability-first selection plus explicit CPU fallback. Dedicated runtime perf-probe scenario wiring remains the separate follow-up already tracked under NBNv2-zwv.6.1.

Added direct PerfProbeRunner regression tests and revalidated with worker-profile --json on the RTX 5090 host plus a full dotnet test pass of 948/948.

title: Implement Real Worker Capability Probing and Benchmark Scores
description: Worker capabilities previously defaulted to placeholder values. This issue now covers real capability probing, explicit benchmark scoring, settings-backed HiveMind ownership of worker benchmark rerun cadence, split GPU compute vs VRAM limits, worker pressure/load reporting for placement and queued rebalance decisions, and the two-prong perf surface (placement-facing worker profiling plus deeper localhost/current-system stress probing) built on shared benchmark/reporting code.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Heartbeats publish non-placeholder RAM/storage/GPU/ILGPU capability data plus explicit worker limit/load metadata when available. 2) CPU/GPU capability scores are computed or configured explicitly, not hardcoded zero. 3) SettingsMonitor inventory and HiveMind placement inputs reflect the updated capability fidelity, including split GPU compute vs VRAM semantics. 4) Worker benchmark rerun cadence is settings-backed and HiveMind-driven rather than a worker launch-only argument. 5) HiveMind filters or rebalances workers when repeated pressure snapshots exceed configured limits, using the normal queued reschedule path. 6) A shared benchmark/reporting core exists and supports both worker profiling and deeper localhost/current-system NBN limit testing. 7) Benchmark execution produces report artifacts that include both charts and tabular summaries. 8) Benchmark scope remains CPU-first now, reserves GPU execution hooks for NBNv2-zwv.5, and auto-skips GPU runtime rows cleanly when unavailable.
close_reason: Implemented settings-backed HiveMind-owned worker capability refresh, split GPU compute/VRAM limits, pressure-aware placement/rebalance, and the updated worker/deep profiling operator surfaces. GPU runtime execution remains tracked separately in NBNv2-zwv.6.1.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
Structured expansion notes (translated from rough planning):

Benchmark focus order
1. Brain-size limits at defined target tick rates, using several target rates rather than a single point.
2. Brain-count limits on one machine.
3. Maximum sustainable tick rate for a moderate workload size (neurons/axons/brains).
4. Add at least one extra scenario if needed to close obvious coverage gaps in the performance envelope.

Execution constraints
- Single PC / localhost only for now.
- Run all scenarios for CPU and GPU paths.
- If GPU/ILGPU is not present or not supported, skip GPU scenarios without failing the suite.

Reporting requirements
- Persist benchmark outputs as reports.
- Reports must include both tables and charts so ceilings/tradeoffs are easy to compare.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Scope clarification (2026-03-15): this issue is now being implemented as a two-prong localhost performance surface with shared benchmarking/reporting code. Prong 1 is real worker profiling and placement-facing capability/throughput coverage for HiveMind inputs. Prong 2 is a separate deeper localhost stress suite that pushes full NBN runtime limits harder (brain-size ceilings, brain-count ceilings, sustainable tick-rate ceilings, plus any additional actionable scenario). Both prongs are CPU-first now and explicitly reserve GPU execution hooks for future NBNv2-zwv.5 completion; GPU-oriented runs must auto-skip cleanly when hardware/runtime support is absent or not yet wired for the workload path.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implementation update (2026-03-15): completed the non-GPU-testing portion as a shared perf-probe surface. WorkerNode now publishes real capability snapshots and explicit CPU/GPU scores via the existing SettingsMonitor heartbeat path instead of placeholder zeros. Added Nbn.Tools.PerfProbe with worker-profile, localhost-stress, and current-system profile modes plus JSON/CSV/Markdown/HTML report artifacts. Added Workbench Orchestrator 'Profile Current System' launch flow to run the attached profile mode against the currently configured SettingsMonitor coordinates. GPU runtime benchmark rows are present as explicit skips until the RegionShard GPU backend from NBNv2-zwv.5 is available; actual GPU execution remains pending that backend work.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up (2026-03-15): added dedicated Worker benchmark refresh control to Workbench Orchestrator local launch, changed perf-probe stdout to report absolute output paths, added default HTML auto-open with --no-open-report opt-out for automation, expanded HTML/Markdown tables to surface full metrics (including RAM/storage/VRAM), clamped chart labels inside the visible SVG area, and fixed the Windows RAM/storage probe path so worker-profile reports now show nonzero RAM/storage on this host.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implementation update (2026-03-15): moving worker capability rerun ownership into Settings-backed HiveMind orchestration; WorkerNode now publishes raw capability plus explicit limit/load metadata; GPU compute and VRAM limits are split; HiveMind placement filters pressured workers, uses compute-score and VRAM-fit separately, and triggers queued pressure-based reschedules after repeated threshold violations. Workbench now exposes a dedicated Worker Policy surface (settings-backed refresh/pressure controls) instead of a launch-only benchmark-refresh arg. GPU runtime perf execution remains pending NBNv2-zwv.5 / NBNv2-zwv.6.1.

title: Execute GPU runtime perf probe suite after ILGPU backend lands
description: Run the perf-probe GPU runtime rows that are currently emitted as explicit skips. Scope: once the RegionShard GPU backend from NBNv2-zwv.5 is available, execute and validate GPU worker-profile and runtime benchmark scenarios, confirm current-system profiling reflects real GPU runtime readiness, and update docs/issues with actual GPU ceilings rather than skip semantics.
acceptance_criteria: 1) PerfProbe GPU runtime rows execute instead of skipping when NBNv2-zwv.5 is complete and supported hardware exists. 2) Worker-profile and localhost/current-system GPU report artifacts are validated on at least one supported machine. 3) Any runtime/placement issues found during GPU perf execution are fixed in runtime first, not normalized away in tests or reports.
close_reason: GPU runtime perf rows now execute on supported ILGPU hardware, localhost/current-system perf attach path regressions were fixed, and the RTX 5090 host validation is green.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Progress update: implemented and validated groundwork for the GPU runtime perf follow-up, including real Workbench CPU/GPU score chips, a current-system attached-client bind/advertise fix, localhost harness storage-limit isolation for perf runs, a shard backend execution query path, and a WorkerNode nonhost PID normalization fix. The issue is not complete yet: live localhost-stress GPU runtime execution is still blocked by an in-process harness/runtime bug where IO Gateway resolves the signal router PID but then cannot keep the input coordinator PID alive or reachable, leading to repeated EnsureBrainEntry failures and tick timeouts. Leaving NBNv2-zwv.6.1 open pending that runtime fix and real GPU localhost-stress artifact validation on the RTX 5090 host.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Resolved the localhost GPU runtime perf blocker. Fixes included: stable named local HiveMind/Worker actors in PerfProbe, real observed worker actor PIDs for hosted control/shard reporting, HiveMind tick dispatch restored to controller-first delivery, addressed HiveMind tick sinks for hosted shards, and perf-harness runtime config disabling plasticity/homeostasis so GPU rows execute on supported ILGPU hardware instead of silently falling back. Validation on the RTX 5090 host: live built PerfProbe worker-profile completed with 4/4 passed; live built PerfProbe localhost-stress completed with 12/12 passed; full test suite passed 960/960; git fsck --no-progress passed. Current-system attach/bind routing regression was already fixed earlier in this issue line and remains covered by tests.

title: Align Tick Cost Model With Function Weights + Remote Cost Knobs
description: Current cost accounting increments coarse counters and hardcodes remote cost to 0. Implement Appendix-B weighted function costs and configurable remote transport cost terms (`remote_cost_per_batch`, `remote_cost_per_contribution`).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Accum/activation/reset costs incorporate configured per-function/per-tier weights. 2) Remote batch/contribution costs can be toggled/configured and reported in `CostRemote`. 3) Tests validate expected cost deltas for representative function mixes and remote-delivery scenarios.
close_reason: Implemented and validated

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Implemented weighted function cost model + remote cost knobs + unified internal cost/energy flag. Added proto/settings/runtime fanout updates, backend cost calculations, and coverage tests (cost model + runtime propagation). Full build/test passed (533 tests), docs rendered/check clean. Commit: 84f8567.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Follow-up fixes for 100% rollout: (1) Workbench Viz mini-chart now rebases window on cadence changes to avoid stale flat-left segments, (2) debug stream subscription/settings persistence fixed so warn+ diagnostics flow even when Debug panel not selected, (3) Io panel selected-brain cost/energy/plasticity commands serialized to avoid racey out-of-order toggles, (4) runtime now logs cost/energy override + depletion events for troubleshooting. Tests added for cadence rebase + debug setting persistence; full dotnet test Release passed (535).

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Workbench follow-up: Visualizer now surfaces selected-brain energy summary (remaining/rate/last tick cost/cost+energy state) and refreshes it from BrainInfo on selection changes plus throttled tick/stream updates. Added Viz panel interaction test coverage for summary refresh behavior.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text: Adjusted defaults per rollout feedback: system cost+energy master now defaults disabled (cost_energy.system.enabled=false) and per-brain default preference now unsuppressed (CostEnergyEnabled=true). Updated HiveMind/Workbench defaults, SettingsMonitor defaults, docs (section 07 + rendered NBNv2.md), and tests (settings defaults, IO panel defaults, HiveMind runtime/snapshot tests). Full Release test suite passes (537).

title: Complete Plasticity Policy Surface (Delta + Auto Rebase Thresholds)
description: Plasticity runtime supports enable/rate/probabilistic updates, but spec also requires distinct plasticity-delta behavior and threshold-triggered rebasing. Implement the missing config and runtime behavior, and document the resulting operator/runtime semantics.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Plasticity config supports distinct update-delta semantics consistent with the NBN spec (no doc downscope substitute). 2) Automatic rebase trigger supports changed-code count and percent thresholds with deterministic behavior. 3) Tests cover threshold-triggered rebase behavior and overlay reset semantics. 4) NBNv2_Design.md and operator-facing docs are updated to describe implemented plasticity/rebase controls and behavior.
close_reason: Implemented delta-based plasticity semantics, deterministic auto-rebase thresholds, and full runtime/proto/doc/test coverage.

comments.author: Hailey Collet
comments.text:
﻿Completed implementation for NBNv2-zwv.8 (plasticity policy surface).

Delivered runtime/proto behavior:
- Added `plasticity_delta`, `plasticity_rebase_threshold`, and `plasticity_rebase_threshold_pct` across control and IO protocol surfaces.
- Implemented distinct semantics in RegionShard CPU backend:
  - probabilistic gate uses `plasticity_rate`
  - update magnitude uses `plasticity_delta`
- Added deterministic threshold-triggered automatic rebase:
  - count trigger on changed runtime-vs-base strength-code count
  - percent trigger on changed/total fraction
  - rebase promotes runtime codes to base codes and clears overlay markers.
- Added compatibility default: when delta is omitted/0, effective delta defaults to plasticity_rate.

Quality gates:
- `dotnet build -c Release --disable-build-servers` passed.
- `dotnet test -c Release --disable-build-servers` passed (487/487).
- Added/updated tests for runtime algorithm deltas, threshold rebase/reset semantics, IO validation + fallback behavior, HiveMind propagation, and proto compatibility.
- Updated canonical docs sections and regenerated `docs/NBNv2.md`.

title: Harden NBN/NBS Validation Invariants
description: Validation gaps remain in `NbnBinaryValidator`: destination span checks are bypassed, stride-zero can crash instead of returning a validation error, and reserved-field/ordering strictness is incomplete.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Axon target neuron IDs are validated against destination region span during `.nbn` validation. 2) Invalid stride produces structured validation errors (no divide-by-zero crash). 3) Reserved bytes/flags and required ordering rules are validated per spec.
close_reason: Closed
